英文原文: The Orthling led the colonists of Beinland against King Volbjorn during the wars of unification. After their defeat they bowed to the inevitable, and offered their chief's daughter, Asgotha, as bride to the king's younger and far more popular brother Surnir. But the marriage, despite its inauspicious origins, turned out well. Asgotha was a shield maiden, and the couple fought side-by-side in battles, up until the day that Surnir was slain in a skirmish with the Sturgians. The new king Halthdar has confirmed her as jarl of Beinland, but many of her kinsmen chaff under the humiliation of Skylfing rule.
中文译文: 统一战争中，奥斯林率领贝因兰殖民者对抗沃尔比约恩·亨格里科农格。战败后，他们屈服于命运，将酋长的女儿阿斯戈萨献给科农格那位更年轻、更得人心的弟弟苏尔尼尔。然而，这桩始于不祥的婚姻却意外美满。阿斯戈萨本是盾女，夫妻二人并肩作战，直至苏尔尼尔在与斯特吉亚人的一次冲突中阵亡。新科农格哈尔斯达尔确认她为贝因兰女雅尔，但她的许多族人仍因受斯凯尔芬统治之辱而愤懑不平。

英文原文: Were Halthdar the Golden a petty chieftain or jarl, he would no doubt be beloved of all the warriors who followed him into battle and sat at his feast-table afterwards, to be showered with praise and rings. But he is a king, in a land that is still unhappy to be a kingdom. He is blamed by his jarls for the sins of his father, Volbjorn the Hungry, who created the Nordvyg with blood and trickery, and many long to return to the old days when they answered to no one.
中文译文: 若哈尔斯达尔·戈尔登仅是小酋长或雅尔，他无疑会深受追随他征战、赴宴并获赠赞美与指环的勇士们爱戴。但他是科农格，统治着一片仍不甘为王国之地。雅尔们因他父亲沃尔比约恩·亨格里的罪孽而责怪他——其父以鲜血与诡计创建诺德维格。许多人渴望回归旧日，那时他们无需向任何人臣服。

英文原文: Grykka Half-an-Ear came from a poor offshoot of the Kjoldings, and was little thought-of until the night that his uncle the jarl and his followers were trapped in their mead-hall by unknown warriors and the roof set alight. His hasty confirmation as jarl by the old king Volbjorn, left little doubt that he was the culprit, and had been suborned by the ruthless unifier of the Nordvyg. Despite this, or perhaps because of it, he is viewed with suspicion by Volbjorn's son and heir Halthdar.
中文译文: 半耳格里卡出身克约尔丁家族一支贫寒旁系，本不受重视，直至一夜，其叔父雅尔及随从被不明武士困于蜜酒厅，屋顶遭纵火。老科农格沃尔比约恩·亨格里匆忙确认他为雅尔，几乎无疑他就是元凶，且已被诺德维格冷酷的统一者收买。尽管如此——或许正因如此——沃尔比约恩之子兼继承人哈尔斯达尔对他心存猜疑。

英文原文: Ilrika was the wife of Volbjorn the Hungry, first King of the Nordvyg. She was a caretaker of a woodland shrine, the keeper of secret rituals, and every bit as ambitious as her husband. When Volbjorn died, Ilrika's machinations ensured that her son, Halthdar, had no serious rivals for the throne. When his rule was secure, she soon died - or so the royal household claimed. Others say she returned to the woods, and some claim to see her wandering on moonless nights, ensuring none dare challenge the dynasty.
中文译文: 伊尔丽卡是沃尔比约恩·亨格里之妻，诺德维格首位科农格后。她守护林中圣所，执掌秘仪，野心不亚于丈夫。沃尔比约恩死后，伊尔丽卡施展权谋，确保其子哈尔斯达尔王位无重大对手。待其统治稳固，她便很快去世——至少王室如此宣称。另有人说她回归森林，有人声称在无月之夜见她游荡，确保无人胆敢挑战王朝。

英文原文: Many over the years have styled themselves King of the Nordvyg, but Volbjorn the Hungry is the first for whom it was more than an empty title. As a young man he served the Empire as a mercenary, accumulating a huge horde of gold. With it, he returned home to transform his jarldom into a kingdom. Some who resisted him were defeated in battle, others were bought, and others found out that someone whom they trusted could be bought. It is whispered that, when Volbjorn was on his deathbed, he ordered himself sealed alive inside his barrow with his gold, so that greed and sorcery could transform him, and he could count it every night.
中文译文: 多年来，许多人自封诺德维格科农格，但沃尔比约恩·亨格里是首位使这称号名副其实之人。年轻时，他作为雇佣兵效力帝国，积攒了巨额黄金。带着财富，他返回故土，将他的雅尔领地变为王国。抵抗者或战败，或被收买，或发现所信之人亦可被收买。传言说，沃尔比约恩临终时，命人将他活活封入坟冢，与黄金同葬，以便贪婪与巫术将他转化，使他每夜都能清点财富。

英文原文: I heard you fought ship to ship with {DEFEATED_PARTY_LEADER}, and won.
中文译文: 我听说你与{DEFEATED_PARTY_LEADER}舰船对舰船交战，并赢得了胜利。

英文原文: I will not forget how our ships met those of {DEFEATED_PARTY_LEADER}, and bested them.
中文译文: 我不会忘记我们的舰船如何遭遇{DEFEATED_PARTY_LEADER}的舰队，并击败了他们。

英文原文: We did well to defeat those pirates back there. The common enemy of mankind, so they say.
中文译文: 我们干得漂亮，击退了那些海盗。他们可是全人类的公敌，人们都这么说。

英文原文: I hear you seized one of my trading vessels. I am not pleased.
中文译文: 我听说你扣押了我的一艘商船。我很不高兴。

英文原文: I remember when we last met and battled, ship to ship.
中文译文: 我记得我们上次相遇交战，舰船对舰船。

英文原文: I remember when you and I last fought together. We had good ships and valiant crews. It's a pity we did not prevail.
中文译文: 我记得你和我上次并肩作战。我们有好的舰船和英勇的船员。可惜我们没能获胜。

英文原文: I shall mark down your name. Now, do not take it amiss if I do not invite you to dine on board, for the world is a treacherous place, and I have learned to be wary.
中文译文: 我会记下你的名字。现在，如果我不邀请你上船用餐，请别见怪，因为这世道险恶，我已学会谨慎。

英文原文: You can't outrun us. Yield or die.[ib:warrior][if:convo_aggressive]
Strike your sails and ship your oars, or we shall attack.[ib:warrior][if:convo_angry]
中文译文: 降帆收桨，否则我们将发起攻击。[ib:warrior][if:convo_angry]

英文原文: Heave to and prepare to be boarded, or fight.[ib:warrior][if:convo_angry]
中文译文: 停船准备接舷，否则开战。[ib:warrior][if:convo_angry]

英文原文: Right. So unless you ship your oars now and let us board you, you're going down.[ib:warrior][if:convo_angry]
中文译文: 好。所以除非你现在收桨让我们登船，否则你就要沉了。[ib:warrior][if:convo_angry]

英文原文: Ahoy there! There's a tax on ships in these waters, sailor, paid either in coin or in blood.[if:convo_mocking_revenge]
中文译文: 喂！在这片水域，舰船都要交税，水手，要么付钱，要么流血。[if:convo_mocking_revenge]

英文原文: You! Heave to, you bastards, or we'll cut every last one of your throats.[if:convo_bared_teeth]
中文译文: 你！停船，你们这些混蛋，否则我们就把你们一个个割喉。[if:convo_bared_teeth]

英文原文: Ahoy there. Furl your sails and ship your oars.[if:convo_mocking_revenge]
Oh aye, you can buy your lives. I wouldn't push too hard on the price if I were you, though, as you might say it's a bit of a seller's market.[ib:hip][rf:convo_bemused]
中文译文: 哦，当然，你们可以买命。不过，我要是你，就不会在价钱上太较劲，毕竟这可是卖方市场。[ib:hip][rf:convo_bemused]

英文原文: Name the sum, and be quick about it, or we'll just feed you to the sharks.[ib:hip][rf:convo_bared_teeth]
中文译文: 报个数，快点，不然就把你们喂鲨鱼。[ib:hip][rf:convo_bared_teeth]

英文原文: Good, good. I much prefer a straight-up deal to rummaging through your gut afterward to see what you might have hid there.[ib:hip][rf:convo_bemused]
中文译文: 好，好。我更喜欢干脆的交易，而不是事后翻你们的肠子找藏货。[ib:hip][rf:convo_bemused]

英文原文: Very well. Sail safely, and don't forget to put aside some silver for your return trip.[ib:closed][rf:convo_bemused]
中文译文: 很好。一路顺风，别忘了留些银币回程。[ib:closed][rf:convo_bemused]

英文原文: We'll take it. Now best get your sails over the horizon before we change your minds.[ib:closed][rf:convo_bared_teeth]
中文译文: 我们收下了。趁我们还没改主意，最好赶紧扬塞尔消失在地平线上。[ib:closed][rf:convo_bared_teeth]

英文原文: Good. Now be careful out there. World's full of thieves, not all of them as kind as we are.[ib:closed][rf:convo_bemused]
中文译文: 好。路上小心。世上多的是贼，不是所有都像我们这么仁慈。[ib:closed][rf:convo_bemused]

英文原文: Well, let no one say we didn't give you a chance.[ib:aggressive][if:convo_annoyed]
中文译文: 好吧，别让人说我们没给过你机会。[ib:aggressive][if:convo_annoyed]

英文原文: Really? I suppose I'll be getting my hands dirty today, then.[ib:aggressive][if:convo_bared_teeth]
中文译文: 是吗？看来今天我得亲自动手了。[ib:aggressive][if:convo_bared_teeth]

英文原文: The Nords are a people made by the sea. The very name of their home, the Nordvyg, refers to the icy stretch of glacier-cut coastline that links their scattered settlements. Until recently, their towns were little more than a shrine or market beside a fjord or estuary, surrounded by a palisade. Some make a modest living by farming barley and raising sheep, but those with ambition must venture forth in their longships to trade, plunder or fight for hire.
中文译文: 诺德人是海洋造就的民族。他们的家园诺德维格之名，正是指冰川切割海岸形成的冰寒峡湾，连接着他们散落的定居点。直到不久以前，他们的城镇不过是峡湾或河口旁的神殿或集市，周围围着木栅栏。有些人靠种植大麦和养羊勉强度日，但有野心的人必须乘长船出海，去贸易、掠夺或受雇作战。

英文原文: I am {CONVERSATION_CHARACTER.LINK}, of the {CLAN_NAME}, one of the ancient lineages of the Nords.
中文译文: 我是{CONVERSATION_CHARACTER.LINK}的{CLAN_NAME}，诺德人古老血脉之一。

英文原文: I am {CONVERSATION_CHARACTER.LINK}, of the {CLAN_NAME}. One table in the High Hall is reserved for our fallen, and two more for those we have slain.
中文译文: 我是{CLAN_NAME}的{CONVERSATION_CHARACTER.LINK}。高厅里有一桌留给我们的逝者，还有两桌留给我们所杀之人。

英文原文: I am {CONVERSATION_CHARACTER.LINK}. I will not boast to you of my lineage. A true son of the North aims to surpass his fathers, not live off of their reputation.
中文译文: 我是{CONVERSATION_CHARACTER.LINK}。我不会向你夸耀我的血统。真正的北方之子志在超越父辈，而非靠他们的名声过活。

英文原文: I am {CONVERSATION_CHARACTER.LINK}, of the {CLAN_NAME}. We are wound-smiths and wave-riders, raven-feeders and ring-breakers.
中文译文: 我是{CLAN_NAME}的{CONVERSATION_CHARACTER.LINK}。我们是伤口工匠与波浪骑手，渡鸦饲者与指环破碎者。

英文原文: I am {CONVERSATION_CHARACTER.LINK}, of the {CLAN_NAME}. It is unseemly to boast, so I will leave it to others to tell you of our deeds. It is a conversation best had on a long winter's night, for it may take some considerable time.
中文译文: 我是{CONVERSATION_CHARACTER.LINK}的{CLAN_NAME}。自夸有失体面，所以还是让别人来告诉你我们的功绩吧。这话题最好留到漫长的冬夜，因为可能要讲上很久。

英文原文: Some day I hope to tell my story to a dozen of my sons, as we feast for the solstice in my hall, so that they shall follow my example and sail out into the world to make their fortunes. Until then, I will settle for telling it to strangers in taverns like yourself.
中文译文: 有朝一日，我希望在我的厅堂里，在冬至宴上，向我的十几个儿子讲述我的故事，让他们效仿我，扬帆出海去闯荡。在那之前，我只好在酒馆里讲给像你这样的陌生人听。

英文原文: I was the youngest in my family. My mother always told me that some day I would own the finest galley in the northlands. My oldest brother heard this, and would mock me incessantly. When I would set forth on my fine ship? Would my mother sail with me to fight my enemies? Perhaps he wished me to shame me into leaving, so I would give him no trouble about our inheritance.
中文译文: 我是家里最小的。母亲总说，总有一天我会拥有北方最棒的桨帆船。我大哥听了这话，就不断嘲笑我。什么时候我才能乘上我的好船？母亲会和我一起出海作战吗？也许他想羞辱我，逼我离开，这样就不会在继承问题上给我添麻烦。

英文原文: One day, soon after I came of age, I took a hundred sheep from our flocks, sold them, and bought a small snekya. I hired some men too, and set off to raid the coast of Sturgia. But of course I was a poor judge of warriors, and when the boyar's druzhina set upon us they scattered, and burned my ship.
中文译文: 有一天，我刚成年不久，从羊群里拿了一百只羊卖掉，买了条小斯内克船。还雇了些人，出发去劫掠斯特吉亚海岸。但我看人的眼光太差，波雅尔的亲兵一冲过来，他们就四散而逃，烧了我的船。

英文原文: I have sworn that I shall not return home, unless I am indeed standing at the helm of a fine ship that my brother could envy. I have fought for Vlandian barons, imperial archons, even once an noyan of the east. I have fought in many battles, some on land and some at sea. But lords are stingy, and I think none has rewarded me according to my due.
中文译文: 我发誓，除非我确实站在一艘让大哥嫉妒的好船的舵轮旁，否则绝不回家。我为瓦兰迪亚男爵、帝国执政官、甚至东方的诺颜打过仗。我参加过许多战斗，有的在陆地，有的在海上。但领主们都很吝啬，我觉得没人按我的功劳奖赏过我。

英文原文: Nothing makes a finer warrior than a hunger to prove yourself that can never be satisfied.
中文译文: 没有什么比永不满足的证明自己的渴望更能造就优秀的战士。

英文原文: I wonder how many men you have slain to forget a child's taunt.
中文译文: 我好奇你杀了多少人，才能忘记儿时的嘲弄。

英文原文: So... Perhaps you have a need of warriors, or captains for your ships. If you are a generous lord, then I can be a worthy follower.
中文译文: 所以……也许你需要战士，或者船长。如果你是个慷慨的领主，那我就是个值得追随的人。

英文原文: I was the youngest son in my family. Well, in our lands, sons - especially the youngest - are expected to make their own name. I can fight by land or by sea, and I am ready to serve whoever rewards me well.
中文译文: 我是家中最小的儿子。在我们那里，儿子——尤其是最小的——必须自己闯出名号。我陆战海战皆可，谁出价高，我便为谁效力。

英文原文: I seek work. I will tell you my story, so that you are in no doubt of my skills.
中文译文: 我寻一份差事。且听我道来，你便知我本事如何。

英文原文: I come from Svalfjord. Farther north than that, people cannot live. There is ice in our bay for six months of the year. In the summer we hunt whales, black whales, which are smaller than most but are cunning and can still sink you if you're not careful. Each summer we gather in our boats and drive the whales into the fjord. Since I was a girl I would go with my father and uncle and several others from the village.
中文译文: 我来自斯瓦尔峡湾。再往北，便无人能居。海湾一年有六个月结冰。夏日我们猎鲸，黑鲸——比多数鲸鱼小，却狡诈异常，稍不留神便能掀翻你的船。每年夏天，我们聚船驱鲸入峡。自少女时起，我便随父亲、叔父和村里人一同出海。

英文原文: There was a dispute over a whale carcass. I do not remember what caused it, exactly, but my father felt he had sole claim to it. He cursed the others and said words that could not be unsaid. He would not apologize, and had to leave the village. So it was just me, him and my uncle, living alone on the coast, in a shelter dug into the turf with a hole in the roof to let out smoke.
中文译文: 曾因一头鲸尸起争执。具体缘由我已记不清，但父亲认为那鲸独属于他。他咒骂众人，说了些覆水难收的话。他不肯道歉，只得离村。于是只剩我、父亲和叔父三人，独居海岸，住在一间挖进草皮、屋顶开洞排烟的棚屋里。

英文原文: My father insisted that we would still hunt. It was madness, just three people in a boat, but what else could we do? One day a whale that he harpooned dragged us far out to sea. When we were struggling to row back, a storm blew up, and sank the boat. My father and I swam ashore but my uncle drowned. I'd had enough. I left the next day.
中文译文: 父亲执意继续捕鲸。三人一船，简直是疯了，可我们又能如何？一日，他叉中的鲸将我们拖入远海。我们奋力回划时，风暴骤起，船沉了。父亲与我游上岸，叔父却溺亡。我受够了。次日便离开了。

英文原文: Some men persist in folly unto death, and there is nothing you can do.
中文译文: 有些人执迷不悟至死，你毫无办法。

英文原文: Where I come from, parents are to be honored and obeyed.
中文译文: 在我家乡，父母当受尊敬与服从。

英文原文: So... I can cast a harpoon, and handle a boat that is being dragged by a whale in its death throes. I think I would be of use in any ship's crew, if the pay is right.
中文译文: 所以……我能掷鱼叉，也能在被垂死之鲸拖拽的船上掌舵。若报酬合适，我想任何船上的水手我都做得来。

英文原文: I am from the Svalfjord, the further reaches of the Nordlands. We hunt whales in small boats. We trust our lives to each other's skill. If you have a ship to crew, you will find no one better.
中文译文: 我来自斯瓦尔峡湾，诺德兰的偏远之地。我们乘小舟猎鲸，性命相托于彼此技艺。若你有船需人，再找不到比我更好的了。

英文原文: I was born in a farm near Throndelag, but my family moved to the new lands near the Jumne river. If I see neither place again as long as I live, I will have no regret.
中文译文: 我生于特隆德拉格附近的一座农场，但家人迁居到了尤姆讷河畔的新地。若此生再不见这两处地方，我也毫无遗憾。

英文原文: We moved to the Jumne because we heard there was farmland for the taking. When we arrived, we found there were Vanni already there, but at first they did not mind our presence. They lived in the woods and left us the open meadows by the river. I was given in marriage to the Vanni chieftain's son to seal the arrangement, along with some cloth and axe-heads.
中文译文: 我们迁往尤姆讷，是因听说那里有田可占。抵达时，发现瓦尼人已在彼处，但起初他们并不介意。他们居于林中，将河畔的开阔草地留给我们。为缔结协议，我连同一些布匹和斧头被许配给瓦尼酋长之子。

英文原文: More Nords followed, however. I was living in my husband's village, and the Vanni complained that the coming and going ruined the hunting. They thought this voided the deal. There were talks, but they went nowhere. So the Vanni killed some Nords in the fields - though not my brothers, as a consideration to me.
中文译文: 然而，更多的诺德人接踵而至。我住在丈夫的村子里，瓦尼人抱怨人来人往破坏了狩猎。他们认为这使协议作废。双方谈判，却无果而终。于是瓦尼人在田里杀了一些诺德人——但念及我，未动我的兄弟。

英文原文: The Vanni hoped the other Nords would flee. But they did not know them. One of my brothers came to me in secret. He told me to poison my Vanni husband's family, like I was that old queen of Sturgland. Hah! One day my family demands that I wed this man and have his children, the next they say to kill him? I wasn't having it. I slipped off that night, and wish to see neither brothers nor husband again.
中文译文: 瓦尼人指望其他诺德人会逃走。但他们不了解诺德人。我的一位兄弟秘密来找我，让我毒死瓦尼丈夫的家人，就像那位斯特吉兰的老女王一样。哈！一天家人要我嫁与此人生儿育女，第二天又让我杀他？我才不干。当夜我便溜走，兄弟和丈夫，我一个也不想再见。

英文原文: Yes, certainly family can be demanding...
中文译文: 是啊，家人有时确实苛求……

英文原文: Hmf. Without kin, we are as beasts.
At any rate, a year with the Vanni has taught me much about the woods, about herbs, the treatment of wounds and fevers. I reckon I could be of good use to a company of warriors.
中文译文: 无论如何，与瓦尼人相处一年，我学到了许多关于森林、草药、以及治疗创伤和热病的知识。我想我对一支战团会很有用。

英文原文: I am from the Jumne. Although I am Nord by birth, I have lived among the Vanni. I know much about herbs, and the art of healing.
中文译文: 我来自尤姆讷。虽生为诺德人，却曾与瓦尼人同住。我深谙草药与治疗之术。

英文原文: You want to hear a story, eh? I'll tell you the short version. The long, well, that could keep a longhouse entertained for a half-dozen midwinter nights. Beinlandings... In matters of the law, they don't like to make things simple. Or cheap.
中文译文: 想听故事，嗯？我讲个短的。长的嘛，够让长屋里的人乐上六七个仲冬夜。贝因兰人……在律法上，他们从不把事情弄简单——或便宜。

英文原文: See, I came to Beinland some years ago, like many from the Nordvyg. Land for the taking, they said, and all men stand equal before the law. I found one unsettled valley with a stream and lush grass, and built my home there. I should have asked why no one done so before.
中文译文: 瞧，我多年前来到贝因兰，和许多诺德维格人一样。他们说，土地任取，法律面前人人平等。我找到一处无人定居的山谷，有溪流和茂草，便在那里建了家。我本该问问为何之前无人定居。

英文原文: Turns out my neighbor, Odig, was a difficult one. People say he had troll-blood. One day he shows up with his retainers and tells me my sheep had broken into his fields, when they hadn't, and demanded compensation. I told him to go away. Then I started to find my sheep dead in their pastures, and then one of my herdsman. I decided to take my case before the Thing.
中文译文: 结果我的邻居奥迪格是个难缠的家伙。人们说他流着巨魔的血。一天他带着随从出现，说我的羊闯进了他的田地——其实没有——要求赔偿。我让他滚蛋。随后我开始在牧场发现死羊，接着是一个牧人。我决定将案子告到庭会。

英文原文: The Thinglaw, though... To gain a hearing I needed a chieftain to back my case. I found one, but he brought a great retinue along with him, which I had to feast. The Thing ruled in my favor and outlawed Odig - everyone hated him - but to enforce the judgment , to take his head and his property, well, I was on my own there. My chief offered to provide armed men, but not for free.
中文译文: 但庭会律法……要获得审理，我需要一位酋长支持我的案子。我找到一位，但他带了大队随从，我得宴请他们。庭会判我胜诉，将奥迪格逐出法外——人人都恨他——但要执行判决，取他首级、夺他财产，就得靠我自己了。那位首领愿提供武装，但并非免费。

英文原文: Sounds like a fine source of income for the old clans of Beinland.
中文译文: 听起来贝因兰的古老氏族倒是财源滚滚。

英文原文: Just get yourself a lord and master. Much simpler than setting your own course though this world.
中文译文: 找个领主主子吧。比在这世上自己闯荡简单多了。

英文原文: By then I had quite a few debts. Well, I sold my herd and my farmhouse to pay my creditors, and here I am, looking for a new source of income. I'll miss Beinland though. Herding in the lonely moors, hunting in the quiet woods. I like to roam the land, and if you're looking for a man to scout ahead, well, I may be him.
中文译文: 那时我欠了不少债。于是，我卖掉畜群和农舍来还债，如今在此，寻找新的财源。不过我会想念贝因兰的。在荒原上放牧，在幽林中狩猎。我喜欢漫游四方，若你需要一个探路之人，或许我正合适。

英文原文: I'm from the Nordvyg, but moved to Beinland. I bought a herd of sheep there, which I hoped would make my fortune, but between several bad winters and some disputes with my neighbors, well, that all came to naught. I'm much poorer than I was before, but at least I'm a bit wiser for having studied at the foot of such a harsh teacher as that island. If you're looking for someone to lead your company through the wilds, well, I may be him.
中文译文: 我来自诺德维格，但搬到了贝因兰。我在那里买了一群羊，指望以此致富，但几场严冬和邻里纠纷让一切化为乌有。我比从前穷多了，但至少在那座岛屿严酷的教训下变得聪明了些。若你需要一个能带领队伍穿越荒野的人，或许我正合适。

英文原文: So... What am I doing in this dingy tavern, far from my homeland? Well, I suppose it's because I was taught that it's a fine thing to be ambitious, to seize what chances fate gives you...
中文译文: 那么……我为何会在这远离故乡的肮脏酒馆里？嗯，大概是因为我从小被教导，雄心是好事，要抓住命运给予的机会……

英文原文: I was sent by my village as a fyrdman to our jarl, Grykka Half-an-Ear. I was eager to fight and win wealth and glory, but I knew a bit about metalwork and he needed a smith, so instead of joining his warriors they put me in a sooty alcove at the base of the wall and told to pump the bellows and hammer nails and horseshoes.
中文译文: 我被村子派去给雅尔格里卡·半耳当菲尔德民兵。我渴望战斗，赢得财富与荣耀，但我略懂金属活计，而他需要铁匠，于是我没能加入他的战士队伍，反而被安置在城墙底下一个乌黑的壁龛里，整天拉风箱、锤钉子和马蹄铁。

英文原文: But a smith's life has its advantages. The ladies of the castle, you see, sometimes needed their jewelry repaired. I was down there hammering away in the sweat and the smoke, and I suppose I was one of the few interesting things to see in that freezing fortress if you're a jarl's daughter with a great deal of time on her hands.
中文译文: 但铁匠的生活也有好处。城堡里的贵妇们有时需要修理首饰。我在下面汗流浃背、烟雾缭绕地敲打，我想，在那座冰冷的堡垒里，对于一个有大把时间的雅尔女儿来说，我算是少数几个有趣的事物之一。

英文原文: But you know what complications can come of love. The daughter was late in her time, and soon people would be asking questions. I helped myself to a blade and a horse in place of my long-unpaid wages and rode away as fast as I could. Maybe I'll go back 20 years from now and greet the new jarl with a cheerful, 'Hello, son!'
中文译文: 但你知道爱情会带来什么麻烦。那位女儿有了身孕，很快人们就会追问。我拿了一把剑和一匹马，权当久未支付的工钱，然后尽快骑马离去。也许二十年后我会回去，对新雅尔欢快地打个招呼：‘你好，儿子！’

英文原文: I suppose you owed nothing to a master who took you for granted.
中文译文: 我想，对于一个视你为理所当然的主人，你并不欠他什么。

英文原文: Only a scoundrel would boast of such base behavior.
中文译文: 只有无赖才会吹嘘如此卑劣的行径。

英文原文: So, I don't mind sharpening blades and fixing armor, but the one thing I ask is the chance to better myself. Now that I've laid that out straight, what do you say? Do you have work for a man like me?
中文译文: 所以，我不介意磨刀修甲，但我只求一个提升自己的机会。既然我已直言不讳，你怎么说？有活给我这样的人干吗？

英文原文: My father was a smith, and I learned his trade. I know such skills are in high demand. I will gladly sharpen blades and repair armor for my pay, but the one thing I ask from any master is the chance to rise in the world.
中文译文: 我父亲是铁匠，我继承了他的手艺。我知道这类技能很抢手。我乐意磨刀修甲来换取报酬，但我对任何主人只有一个要求：给我一个出人头地的机会。

英文原文: I can only hope that Heaven has prepared a reward for all the sorrow and suffering that I am about to relate to you.
中文译文: 我只能希望上天为我即将讲述的悲伤与苦难准备了奖赏。

英文原文: I am a skilled mariner. I am from Quyaz. The blood of the Kannic people runs in my veins, and Kannic blood is half saltwater, so they say. I took to the sea at an early age as a navigator's apprentice, and by 20 I had accumulated enough to buy a share in a ship sailing the Jade Sea.
中文译文: 我是一名熟练的水手。我来自库亚兹。坎尼克人的血脉在我体内流淌，人们说坎尼克的血一半是海水。我早年便出海，给领航员当学徒，二十岁时攒够了钱，买下了一艘在玉海航行的船的股份。

英文原文: Alas, our captain was a braggart. We were headed to Revyl with a cargo of dates and dyes. He wished to show his knowledge of the Bay of Ortygia, and took a quick route between the islands. Of course he was dashed on the rocks. I seized a timber, and was washed shore with a dozen other half-drowned survivors who had what I guess you could call luck.
中文译文: 唉，我们的船长是个吹牛大王。我们载着椰枣和染料前往雷维尔。他想炫耀自己对奥尔提吉亚湾的了解，便抄近道穿行于岛屿之间。结果船撞上了礁石。我抓住一根木头，和十几个半死不活的幸存者一起被冲上岸——我们大概算是幸运吧。

英文原文: My next voyage was on a felucca out of Zeonica heading eastward to Danustica. Pirates took us, and burnt it to the waterline. Then I took service on a dromon, fitted as a privateer, but we were caught by an unseasonal dust-storm that rolled off the Jabal Tamar and smashed us into a rock in the Gulf of Charas. Each time, destiny saw fit to spare me after first having paupered me.
中文译文: 我的下一次航行是在一艘从泽奥尼卡出发东行至达努斯蒂卡的帆船上。海盗劫持了我们，把船烧到吃水线。随后我上了一艘改装成私掠船的德罗蒙战舰，但一场不合时节的沙尘暴从贾巴尔塔玛尔山席卷而来，将我们撞碎在查拉斯湾的礁石上。每一次，命运都先让我一贫如洗，然后又饶我一命。

英文原文: Great are your sorrows, but greater still your perseverance! Surely it shall be rewarded with riches.
中文译文: 你的苦难深重，但你的坚韧更甚！这必将以财富作为回报。

英文原文: Yeah, okay, maybe also consider that your destiny wants you to remain on dry land.
中文译文: 嗯，好吧，或许也该想想，你的命运是想让你待在陆地上。

英文原文: Despite my sufferings I still yearn for travel and traffic. And none of these wrecks was any fault of my own. Misfortune is a good teacher, they say, and I think my knowledge is second to none in terms of what not to do when in command of a ship.
中文译文: 尽管遭受了这些苦难，我仍渴望旅行和贸易。而且这些海难都不是我的错。人们说，不幸是良师，我认为在指挥船只时不该做什么，我的知识无人能及。

英文原文: I'm a sailor out of Quyaz. We venture further from our homes than anyone else on the coast, and I believe that my knowledge of winds, currents and stars is the equal of any of my peers.
中文译文: 我是来自库亚兹的水手。我们比海岸上任何人都离家更远，我相信我对风、洋流和星辰的了解不逊于任何同行。

英文原文: Let me think how I want to put this...

I'm from Zeonica. In my youth, the archon was one Sulvanis, father of the current Garios. Now, let us say your empire is at peace with the Aserai and Vlandians, but their merchants are cutting into your pockets and you expect to be at war in the future. If one of your mariners sailed out of harbor with one ship and came back with two, would you question them too carefully?
中文译文: 我来自泽奥尼卡。我年轻时，执政官是苏尔瓦尼斯，即现任加里奥斯之父。这么说吧，假设你的帝国与阿塞莱和弗兰迪亚人和平相处，但他们的商人正在掏你的腰包，而你预计未来会开战。如果你的一个水手驾一艘船出港，却带回两艘，你会过于仔细地盘问他吗？

英文原文: So here's where I come in... Let's say you have acquired a cheap Aserai shabak, by one means or the other. It was intended as a ship that could move goods from one place to another as cheaply as possible. But you want it refitted to be faster, with a lead-sheeted belly and some towers for archers, never mind why. My family and I specialized in adding them.
中文译文: 所以我的作用来了……假设你通过某种方式弄到了一艘便宜的阿塞莱沙巴克船。它原本设计为尽可能廉价地运输货物。但你想要改装它，使其更快，加上铅皮船底和弓箭手塔楼——别问为什么。我和我的家族专精于此。

英文原文: But suppose again that you are the archon Garios, scheming to become emperor. You need a treaty with the sultan, sooner not later. And the sultan has his court full of aggrieved merchants. So you announce a list of men to be exiled, names that are known throughout the Perassic Sea as men of ingenuity and daring.
中文译文: 但再假设，你是执政官加里奥斯，正图谋成为皇帝。你需要尽快与苏丹缔结条约，越快越好。而苏丹的宫廷里满是心怀怨愤的商人。于是你宣布一份流放名单，上面都是佩拉海闻名遐迩的智勇之士。

英文原文: No nation ever prospered on the seas without a bit of corsairing.
中文译文: 没有哪个国家能在海上繁荣昌盛而不沾点海盗行径。

英文原文: Pirates, and those that help them, are the common enemy of mankind.
中文译文: 海盗及其帮凶，乃是人类公敌。

英文原文: I will say this. Many lords condemn us, but relatively few are willing to pass up the opportunity to employ us, at least not when they have ships to maintain.
中文译文: 我要说，许多贵族谴责我们，但相对而言，很少有人愿意放弃雇佣我们的机会——至少在他们有船要维护的时候不会。

英文原文: I grew up in Zeonica. Corsairing is an honorable profession there. It hones the skills of the empire's sailors, between wars. I specialize in the construction and modification of corsair vessels, and may even have sailed on one from time to time.
中文译文: 我在泽奥尼卡长大。在那里，海盗是份荣誉的职业。它在战争间隙磨练帝国水手的技艺。我专攻海盗船建造与改装，偶尔也会驾船出海。

英文原文: I am from Charas. I don't know how much you know of its recent past.
中文译文: 我来自查拉斯。不知你对它近来的历史了解多少。

英文原文: My father was one of the Sea-Victuallers. Perhaps you have heard of them. When the guildmaster of Charas brought in the Brotherhood of the Woods and rebelled against the Vlandian king, the fishermen of the coast brought supplies to the besieged city. They did it for pay, mind, as starving cities will pay dearly for moldy bread and minnows, not because they believed a word of the Brotherhood's mad promises of a world without lords and ladies and taxes and tithes.
中文译文: 我父亲曾是“海上粮商”之一。你或许听说过他们。当查拉斯行会首领引入林间兄弟会并反抗弗兰迪亚国王时，沿海渔民为被围困的城市运送补给。他们是为了报酬，要知道，饥饿的城市会为发霉的面包和小鱼付出高昂代价，而不是因为他们相信兄弟会那套关于没有领主、没有贵族、没有税收和什一税的疯狂承诺。

英文原文: But the Baron dey Cortain, who led the siege, wasn't terribly interested in their reasons. He went up and down the coast, hanging anyone with a boat with five leagues of Charas who could not give a full account of himself. An informant put my father's name on the list, and so we like so many others fled to the islands of the gulf.
中文译文: 但指挥围城的德·科坦男爵对他们的动机毫无兴趣。他沿着海岸来回巡视，绞死了所有在查拉斯方圆五里格内拥有船只且无法自证清白的人。一个告密者把我父亲的名字列入了名单，于是我们像许多其他人一样逃到了海湾的岛屿上。

英文原文: So that's where I grew up on, on a gull-splattered bit of rock, living on fish and seaweed and rainwater, because of course the baron's informant had taken my family's land in his absence. But I can safely say that no one knows the sea like I do, as the wind can come at you hard from any direction at any time of day, and a fisherman who can't slip between two rocks a stone's throw apart with a breeze hard on his beam is food for the sharks.
中文译文: 所以我就是在那里长大的，在一块海鸥粪便遍布的礁石上，靠鱼、海藻和雨水为生——因为男爵的告密者趁我父亲不在时夺走了我们家的土地。但我敢说，没人比我更了解大海，因为风随时可能从任何方向猛烈袭来，而一个不能在劲风侧吹时从一石之遥的两块礁石间穿过的渔夫，只会成为鲨鱼的食物。

英文原文: A cruel fate to suffer, just for bring food to your fellow-men.
中文译文: 仅仅因为给同胞送食物就遭受如此残酷的命运。

英文原文: What is it with the fishermen of Charas? Are there no fish left to catch, that they must profit from smuggling and rebellion?
中文译文: 查拉斯的渔民是怎么回事？难道没有鱼可捕了，非得靠走私和叛乱牟利？

英文原文: At any rate, if you are looking for a helmsman, I doubt you will find a better one.
中文译文: 无论如何，如果你在找舵手，我怀疑你找不到比我更好的了。

英文原文: I am from Charas. The islands of the coast are rich in mackeral and tuna but treacherous, with many small currents and unpredictable winds. We reckon ourselves the finest sailors in Calradia, if you don't mind my saying.
中文译文: 我来自查拉斯。沿海岛屿盛产鲭鱼和金枪鱼，但海况凶险，暗流众多，风向无常。我们自认为是卡拉迪亚最优秀的水手，恕我直言。

英文原文: Ahoy! I want to speak to your captain.
中文译文: 嘿！我要和你们的船长说话。

英文原文: Ahoy! Damn your eyes and stay out of our way, you clumsy lubbers. Who captains you?
中文译文: 嘿！该死的，别挡道，你们这些笨手笨脚的旱鸭子。谁在指挥你们？

英文原文: {STR_SALUTATION}... Our ship is at your command.
Who are you? Strike your sails and ship your oars, or prepare to fight.
中文译文: 你是谁？降帆收桨，否则准备战斗。

英文原文: {STR_SALUTATION}... Is that you? Please be brief. The winds are with us and I have no time to waste.
中文译文: {STR_SALUTATION}……是你吗？请长话短说。顺风顺水，我没时间浪费。

英文原文: Ahoy, there! May I have the pleasure of knowing who captains this vessel?
中文译文: 嘿，那边！敢问这艘船的船长是哪位？

英文原文: Ahoy, there! If you could be so kind, could you try to steer your lump of flotsam out of our way?
中文译文: 嘿，那边！劳驾，能不能把你那堆破木头挪开，别挡我们的路？

英文原文: Ahoy! As it appears your poor floundering tub can't outrun us, we might as well have a little chat. Perhaps you'd like to introduce yourself?
中文译文: 嘿！既然你那艘破船跑不过我们，不如聊聊。或许你愿意自我介绍一下？

英文原文: {STR_SALUTATION}... Ahoy! So somehow you've managed to keep yourself afloat since we last met. It's good to see you, you worthless lubber!
中文译文: {STR_SALUTATION}……嘿！你居然还漂在水上，自我们上次见面后。见到你真好，你这没用的旱鸭子！

英文原文: Ahoy! What's your name? Forgive me if we have spoken before and I do not recall, for I have met many promising captains only to hear later they were swallowed by the deep.
中文译文: 嘿！你叫什么名字？如果我们之前说过话而我忘了，请见谅，我见过许多前途无量的船长，后来却听说他们被大海吞没了。

英文原文: Ahoy, there! I know you. Be careful, now, as you might find that you've caught a bigger fish than you first reckoned.
中文译文: 嘿，那边！我认识你。小心点，你可能会发现自己钓到了一条比预想更大的鱼。

英文原文: Ahoy! Greetings to you! May I ask who captains this vessel?
中文译文: 嘿！向你致敬！请问这艘船的船长是谁？

英文原文: Ahoy! Do you care to tell me your name, before I give the order to engage your ship?
中文译文: 啊嗬！在我下令攻击你的船之前，你愿意告诉我你的名字吗？

英文原文: Ahoy! Respect the custom of the sea and the terms of parley, and I shall do likewise. Now, who are you?
中文译文: 啊嗬！尊重海洋的习俗和谈判的条款，我也会如此。现在，你是谁？

英文原文: {STR_SALUTATION}... Pirates like you may put on airs, but they all end up the same way, dangling from the yardarm of their betters.
中文译文: {STR_SALUTATION}……像你这样的海盗或许会装腔作势，但最终都一个下场，吊在比你强的人的桅杆横桁上。

英文原文: {STR_SALUTATION}... Ahoy! I hope you have been enjoying fair weather and good seas.
中文译文: {STR_SALUTATION}……啊嗬！我希望你一直享受着好天气和好海况。

英文原文: {STR_SALUTATION}... Is that you? It doesn't look like you can escape your fate, does it? Let's see if the sea accepts your vile corpse, or if it just vomits it back up.
中文译文: {STR_SALUTATION}……是你吗？看起来你逃不过你的命运，对吧？让我们看看大海是接受你肮脏的尸体，还是把它吐出来。

英文原文: Ahoy! If you can hear me at that distance, come no further. Whose ship is this?
中文译文: 啊嗬！如果你能在那个距离听到我说话，就不要再靠近了。这是谁的船？

英文原文: Ahoy! We know you're an enemy, so do not think to take us unawares.
中文译文: 啊嗬！我们知道你是敌人，所以别想偷袭我们。

英文原文: Ahoy! You appear be a foe, and in a minute I will order my ship to close with you and take you. But first, I would know your name.
中文译文: 啊嗬！你看起来是个敌人，再过一分钟我就会命令我的船靠近你并拿下你。但首先，我想知道你的名字。

英文原文: {STR_SALUTATION}... My ship and crew are yours to command.
中文译文: {STR_SALUTATION}……我的船和船员听候你的指挥。

英文原文: Ahoy! Keep a bowshot's length away, or more, until I know if you're friend or foe.
中文译文: 啊嗬！保持一箭之遥或更远，直到我知道你是敌是友。

英文原文: Ahoy! Who are you? Speak, before I send you to the bottom.
中文译文: 啊嗬！你是谁？说话，否则我把你送到海底。

英文原文: Ahoy! You want something with us? You might find you've caught more than you bargained for.
中文译文: 啊嗬！你想找我们有事？你可能会发现你惹上了不该惹的麻烦。

英文原文: Ahoy! So, who captains this here wallowing sow of a vessel?
中文译文: 啊嗬！那么，谁指挥这头笨重的母猪船？

英文原文: Ahoy! If have anything to say, then say it. But try anything tricky and we'll send you right to the bottom.
中文译文: 啊嗬！如果你有话要说，就说吧。但要是耍花招，我们就把你直接送到海底。

英文原文: Well, I'll not deny it. It'll be a real pleasure to feed you to the sharks.
中文译文: 好吧，我不否认。把你喂鲨鱼真是件乐事。

英文原文: Ahoy! {STR_SALUTATION}... I'd know your vessel anywhere! Look at the state of that ship. I'm surprised it doesn't just sink into the sea out of shame.
中文译文: 啊嗬！{STR_SALUTATION}……我一眼就能认出你的船！看看那船的样子。我很惊讶它没有因为羞愧而沉入大海。

英文原文: Ahoy! ... What ship is this, and who's the captain?
中文译文: 啊嗬！……这是什么船，船长是谁？

英文原文: Ahoy there, now! Before we fight, if you will, tell me who captains your ship!
中文译文: 啊嗬，听着！在我们开打之前，如果你愿意，告诉我谁指挥你的船！

英文原文: {STR_SALUTATION}... Is that you on that ship? It's been a while.
中文译文: {STR_SALUTATION}……在那艘舰船上的是你吗？好久不见了。

英文原文: Ahoy! I know you, don't I? {STR_SALUTATION}... Do you and I have anything to talk about?
中文译文: 啊嗬！我认识你，对吧？{STR_SALUTATION}……你和我有什么好谈的吗？

英文原文: Ahoy! May I speak to your captain?
中文译文: 啊嗬！我能和你的船长谈谈吗？

英文原文: Ahoy! Is that you, {STR_SALUTATION}? It's good to see you.
中文译文: 啊嗬！是你吗，{STR_SALUTATION}？很高兴见到你。

英文原文: Ahoy! Do you wish to speak, before we attack?
中文译文: 啊嗬！在我们进攻之前，你想谈谈吗？

英文原文: Ahoy! I know your ship. {STR_SALUTATION}, isn't it? Do you and I have anything to talk about?
中文译文: 啊嗬！我认识你的舰船。{STR_SALUTATION}，对吧？你和我有什么好谈的吗？

英文原文: I do not know your ship. Who commands you?
中文译文: 我不识得你的舰船。谁在指挥你？

英文原文: Ahoy! Keep your distance, lest you foul our sails. Who is in command over there?
中文译文: 嘿！保持距离，免得你弄脏我们的塞尔。那边是谁在指挥？

英文原文: Greetings! Do I speak to the captain of this ship, my lady?
中文译文: 幸会！莱迪，我是在与这艘舰船的船长说话吗？

英文原文: Who captains this ship? I would know whom I slay.
中文译文: 谁指挥这艘舰船？我要知道我杀的是谁。

英文原文: {STR_SALUTATION}... I shall stain the seas with your blood.
You, over there! With which bold seafarer do I have the pleasure of speaking?
中文译文: 你，那边那位！我有幸与哪位大胆的航海者交谈？

英文原文: You, over there! Tell me the name of the captain brave enough to put to sea in such a leaky jumble of planks as your ship.
中文译文: 你，那边那位！告诉我那位勇敢的船长名字，他竟敢驾着你这艘漏水的木板拼凑物出海。

英文原文: I trust you have been keeping well, enjoying the salt air and sending the miscreants of the sea to their watery graves.
中文译文: 我相信你一直安好，享受着咸腥的海风，将海上的恶徒送入水底坟墓。

英文原文: Well... Unless I reckon the odds falsely, I'd say you have a good chance of getting wet shortly. I hope you can swim.
中文译文: 嗯……除非我算错了胜算，否则我敢说你很快就要湿透了。希望你会游泳。

英文原文: {STR_SALUTATION}! When I saw that hull all covered with barnacles, those tangled ropes, that cluttered deck, I knew it must be you!
中文译文: {STR_SALUTATION}！当我看到那长满藤壶的哈尔、那些缠结的绳索、那杂乱的德克，我就知道一定是你！

英文原文: Peace to you, stranger, and fair seas. I do not know your ship. To whom do I speak?
中文译文: 愿你平安，陌生人，愿你一路顺风。我不识得你的舰船。我在与谁说话？

英文原文: I do not know your ship. Who are you? What do you want with us?
中文译文: 我不识得你的舰船。你是谁？你想对我们做什么？

英文原文: You, over there! Who commands you? Know that we mean to take you or to sink you.
中文译文: 你，那边那位！谁指挥你？要知道我们打算要么夺取你，要么击沉你。

英文原文: {STR_SALUTATION}... Peace to you... I hope the sea air is doing you good.
中文译文: {STR_SALUTATION}……愿你平安……希望海风对你身体有益。

英文原文: {STR_SALUTATION}... The seas have delivered you into our hands.
You, over there! Peace to you! Who captains your ship?
中文译文: 你，那边那位！愿你平安！谁指挥你的舰船？

英文原文: {STR_SALUTATION}... I hope the seas have been treating you well.
I don't know you... If you have anything to say, say it quickly. The sharks are hungry and I would not keep them waiting.
中文译文: 我不认识你……如果你有话要说，就快说。鲨鱼饿了，我不想让它们久等。

英文原文: I've waited a long time for this day. Have a good look around you, for soon your eyes will have little to see but the inside of a shark's belly.
中文译文: 我等这一天已经很久了。好好看看四周，因为很快你的眼睛除了鲨鱼肚子的内部，就什么也看不到了。

英文原文: {SISTER.NAME} is the little sister of {PLAYER.NAME}. She was abducted by bandits and sold to a confederation of pirates and slave-traders known as the Sea Hounds.
中文译文: {SISTER.NAME}是{PLAYER.NAME}的妹妹。她被匪徒绑架，卖给了名为“西霍恩德团”的海盗与奴隶贩子联盟。

英文原文: Nordic Civilian Hat with Fur Brim

中文译文: 北民掠袭赫维号毛皮披肩

英文原文: Nord Hollow Blunt Two Handed Axehead
Nord Hollow Decorated Two Handed Axehead
Nord Etched Broad Battle Axehead with Knotwork Decorations
中文译文: 诺德蚀刻宽战斧头（结饰纹）

英文原文: Nord Etched Battle Axehead with Knotwork Decorations
中文译文: 诺德蚀刻战斧头（结饰纹）

英文原文: Hardened Battle Axe Handle with Etchings

The Nords live in the cold lands beyond the Byalic Sea. From their ancestral homes in the fjords of the Nordvyg, they have migrated west to the island of Beinland and east to the dark forests of the Jumne river. They grow what food they can in the rocky soils of their homeland, but their wealth comes from the sea - from walrus ivory, from whale oil, and from the raiding longships they send forth to harry the peoples of the south.
中文译文: 诺德人居住在比亚里克海彼岸的寒冷之地。他们从诺德维格峡湾的祖居西迁至贝因兰岛，东抵尤姆内河畔的幽暗森林。他们在故土多石的土地上尽力耕作，但财富却来自海洋——海象牙、鲸油，以及他们派往南方劫掠诸族的长船。

英文原文: The wealth of the Nordvyg comes from the sea. The longships sail south bearing walrus ivory, whale oil, and fish to sell - then return to the fjords laden with plunder and captives. Until recently, southerners could take comfort that the half-dozen so-called "kings" of the Nordvyg each controlled little more than a shrine or slave market surrounded by a wooden palisade. A generation ago, however, Volbjorn the Hungry returned from a decade in the empire's Vaegar Guard with riches that he used to bend the northland to his will, forcing its chieftains to pledge themselves as his jarls. Today his sons rule, and though the bond holding this new realm together are weak, they may strengthen them by turning their vassals' attentions to conquest.
中文译文: 诺德维格的财富来自海洋。长船载着海象牙、鲸油和鱼鲜南行售卖，然后满载掠夺物与俘虏返回峡湾。直至不久之前，南方人尚可聊以自慰：诺德维格那六位所谓的“科农格”，每人不过控制着一座木栅环绕的神殿或奴隶市场。然而，一代人之前，沃尔比约恩·亨格里从帝国瓦伊加尔卫队服役十年归来，携巨富降服北方，迫使众酋长宣誓成为他的雅尔。如今他的儿子们统治此地，尽管维系这个新科农格国的纽带尚显脆弱，但他们或许能通过引导封臣转向征服来加强统治。

英文原文: You cannot take this action, as this ship is being used in a quest.
中文译文: 你无法执行此操作，因为这艘船正被用于某个任务。

英文原文: You saved your brothers, and you decided to let {RADAGOS.LINK} go...
中文译文: 你救出了你的兄弟们，并决定放走{RADAGOS.LINK}……

英文原文: You saved your brothers, and you decided to execute {RADAGOS.LINK} because he caused the death of your parents.
中文译文: 你救出了你的兄弟们，并决定处决{RADAGOS.LINK}，因为他导致了你父母的死亡。

英文原文: We found your little brother. But my former partner {HIDEOUT_BOSS.LINK} betrayed me. We came into his camp to negotiate the boy’s release, and he seized us right then and there.[if:convo_angry_voice]
中文译文: 我们找到了你的弟弟。但我以前的搭档{HIDEOUT_BOSS.LINK}背叛了我。我们进入他的营地谈判释放那孩子，他当场就抓住了我们。[if:convo_angry_voice]

英文原文: Yes, we are all fine. {LITTLE_BROTHER.NAME} is scared but fine... We need to be quick and get the hell out of this place.[if:convo_calm_friendly][ib:confident]
中文译文: 是的，我们都很好。{LITTLE_BROTHER.NAME}很害怕，但没事……我们得快点离开这个鬼地方。[if:convo_calm_friendly][ib:confident]

英文原文: I'll take him to the nearest fortress immediately. He will be safe there.
中文译文: 我立刻带他去最近的堡垒。他在那里会很安全。

英文原文: He led us here. Where are my brothers?
中文译文: 他带我们到了这里。我的兄弟们在哪？

英文原文: Well... Looks like we've gotten your brother back to you, so my end of our deal is complete. I'll be making myself scarce now.[ib:hip][if:convo_uncomfortable_voice]
中文译文: 嗯……看来我们已经把你的弟弟还给你了，所以我这边的事已经完成了。我现在就消失。[ib:hip][if:convo_uncomfortable_voice]

英文原文: Perhaps by saving my brother you earned your life back. Very well, go now.
中文译文: 也许救了我的弟弟让你换回了自己的命。很好，走吧。

英文原文: Good Heaven, I’ll need a better description than that. My men have harvested dozens of little brats in this region. Quite good hunting grounds! Sold some of them to some pirates from Ostican, and sent the rest south to a slave market I know, though.[ib:hip][if:convo_snide_voice]
中文译文: 老天，我需要比这更详细的描述。我的人在这片区域抓了不少小鬼。真是个好猎场！有些卖给了奥斯蒂坎的海盗，剩下的则送到了我知道的一个南方奴隶市场。[ib:hip][if:convo_snide_voice]

英文原文: Oh, you'll need my help all right, if you want to get them back - alive, that is. See, my boys have some pretty specific instructions about what to do if there's a rescue attempt...
中文译文: 哦，如果你想把他们活着救回来，你确实需要我的帮助。你看，我的人有非常具体的指示，一旦有人试图营救该怎么做……

英文原文: Also, I’ll be straight with you, seeing as you’ll find out soon enough anyway. If I recall right, we already sold off your sister to those pirates in Ostican. I remember faces well, you see, and one of them looked a bit like you. So… Here’s what I can do for you. With luck, I can help you get your brother back. Your sister – well, I know nothing of ships and the sea, so I couldn’t tell you where they might have taken her. I’m sorry, but I won’t promise you what I can’t deliver.
中文译文: 另外，我跟你直说吧，反正你迟早也会发现。如果我没记错，我们已经把你妹妹卖给了奥斯蒂坎的那些海盗。我记人脸很准，其中有一个长得有点像你。所以……我能为你做的是：运气好的话，我可以帮你找回你弟弟。至于你妹妹——我对船和海一无所知，所以没法告诉你他们可能把她带去了哪里。抱歉，我不会承诺我做不到的事。

英文原文: I have a better idea. We would have a better chance if we split up now. I’ll take {RADAGOS.NAME} and go find the slaver market. You might wish to make inquiries in Ostican. It’s a port town, so they might have sailed on from there.
中文译文: 我有个更好的主意。如果我们现在分头行动，机会更大。我带{RADAGOS.NAME}去找奴隶市场。你也许可以去奥斯蒂坎打听一下。那是个港口城镇，他们可能从那里出海了。

英文原文: In either case, we must be careful not to endanger their lives and it could be better to just buy them. We need to have our purses full for that though.
中文译文: 无论哪种情况，我们都必须小心，不要危及他们的生命，也许直接买下他们更好。不过我们得带够钱。

英文原文: Get on the road now, and if you get a chance, start making inquiries at Ostican for {LITTLE_SISTER.NAME}. Once I locate {LITTLE_BROTHER.NAME}, I'll come find you.[ib:normal][if:convo_calm_friendly]
中文译文: 现在就上路吧，如果有机会，开始在奥斯蒂坎打听{LITTLE_SISTER.NAME}的下落。一旦我找到{LITTLE_BROTHER.NAME}，我会来找你。[ib:normal][if:convo_calm_friendly]

英文原文: The sambuk is the traditional trading dhow of the Perassic Sea redesigned for warfare. It is to Aserai and Khuzait navies what the liburna is to imperial ones - the all-purpose workhorse, fleshing out the line and guarding the flanks in battle, hunting pirates, or protecting convoys of merchantment. The sambuk is distinguished by its very high speed, which also makes it a favorite of corsairs, if they can obtain one.
中文译文: 桑布克号是佩拉西亚海传统的贸易三角帆船，经过重新设计用于战争。它在阿塞莱和库赛特海军中的地位，如同利布尔纳在帝国海军中一样——是一种多用途的主力船型，在战斗中充实战线、护卫侧翼、追捕海盗或保护商船队。桑布克号以其极高的速度而著称，这也使其成为海盗的最爱——如果他们能弄到一艘的话。

英文原文: Varagos sits on the Aris River, which flows into the deep pool known as the Ophthys. Landowners here have planted mulberry bushes on the warm hillsides, on which they cultivate silkworms.
中文译文: 瓦拉戈斯坐落于阿里斯河畔，该河汇入被称为奥菲斯深潭的水域。当地地主在温暖的山坡上种植桑树，用以养蚕。

英文原文: Lochana is named for a castle that guards the Carcasos river. Along with Epinosa, its twin on the opposite bank, it forms the centuries-old border defense of this far-flung corner of empire against raiders from the north. The nearby sea tempers the cold of the northern winters here, allowing the cultivation of mulberry bushes for silk.
中文译文: 洛卡纳因守护卡尔卡索斯河的城堡而得名。它与对岸的姊妹城埃皮诺萨共同构成了帝国这片偏远角落抵御北方掠夺者、延续数百年的边境防线。附近的海水缓和了北方冬日的严寒，使得桑树得以生长，用于产丝。

英文原文: Rhesos sits amid the jumbled ridges of the Lesser Dryatic Mountains. Highland cattle thrive on the grasses of the heights.
中文译文: 雷索斯坐落于小德亚提克山脉的崎岖山脊之间。高地牛群在高原的草地上繁衍生息。

英文原文: Gaos sits in a broad valley in the Myzead, the rugged highlands in the east of the Empire. Nomadic herders once occupied this land, and the village's name is taken from their word for cattle.
中文译文: 加奥斯位于帝国东部崎岖高地迈泽亚的宽阔山谷中。游牧牧民曾占据此地，村庄的名字便取自他们语言中表示牛群的词汇。

英文原文: Atrion lies by the delta of the Nevys, a cold, slow-moving river that drains into Lake Laconis. The land here was mostly marsh until a well-financed consortium of imperial gentry built dykes to reclaim it. Today they cultivate mulberry bushes, used to produce silk.
中文译文: 阿特里翁位于内维斯河三角洲旁，这条冰冷缓慢的河流注入拉科尼斯湖。此地原多为沼泽，直到一个资金雄厚的帝国士绅联合体修筑堤坝将其开垦。如今他们种植桑树，用以生产丝绸。

英文原文: Ataconia sits near the headwaters of the Aris river in the piedmont of the Dryatic Mountains. The hills, rich with oak trees, are ideal for the raising of acorn-eating swine.
中文译文: 阿塔科尼亚位于德亚提克山脉山麓的阿里斯河源头附近。橡树茂密的山丘是饲养食橡子猪的理想之地。

英文原文: Epinosa sits on a tongue of land surrounded by rivers, the names of which reflect the long history of trade and conflict in this vital crossroads. The northern branch is known as the Caracasos, a name which derives from the Sturgian Karakaz, which itself derives from a Khuzait language. The Nords know it as the Jumne, a word derived from the Sturgian word for barrow. The southern branch is known to all the peoples of this region as the Davuna. The villagers here raise cattle on the thick grass of the riverside marches.
中文译文: 埃皮诺萨坐落于河流环绕的舌形土地上，河流的名称反映了这个重要十字路口悠久的贸易与冲突历史。北支流被称为卡拉卡索斯河，其名源自斯特吉亚语的卡拉卡兹，而卡拉卡兹又源自库赛特语。诺德人称其为尤姆内河，该词源于斯特吉亚语中表示古冢的词汇。南支流被此地区所有民族称为达武纳河。这里的村民在河畔沼泽茂密的草地上放牧牛群。

英文原文: Pons was named for a bridge across the Caracasos River, though that was washed out generations ago in a flood and replaced by other crossings both upstream and downstream. The locals mine deposits of salt from nearby hills.
中文译文: 庞斯因卡拉卡索斯河上的一座桥而得名，尽管那座桥在几代前的一场洪水中被冲毁，取而代之的是上下游的其他渡口。当地人在附近的山丘开采盐矿。

英文原文: Syratos sits at the the base of Mount Tarnea, west of Lake Tanaesis. Flax grows well here, in the cold damp valley floor.
中文译文: 西拉托斯位于塔纳埃西斯湖以西的塔尔内亚山脚下。亚麻在这寒冷潮湿的山谷底部生长良好。

英文原文: Tememos lies where the Plain of Ichala rises to meet the highlands of the Myzead. Horses graze on the mountain slopes in the summers and are taken to the valley floor when grasses sproud there after the winter rains.
中文译文: 泰梅莫斯位于伊查拉平原与迈泽亚高地相接之处。夏季马匹在山坡上吃草，冬季雨后山谷草地萌发时，它们便被带到谷底。

英文原文: Mecalovea sits on a rocky shelf of the Dryatic Mountains, surrounded on three sides by the Miron River and its tributary streams. This site affords it some protection from the raiders and brigands who plague this frontier region. The fast-flowing water has cut through the rock to expose rich seams of iron ore.
中文译文: 梅卡洛维亚坐落于德亚提克山脉的岩石台地上，三面被米隆河及其支流环绕。这一位置为其提供了一定的保护，使其免受侵扰这片边境地区的掠夺者和匪徒的袭击。湍急的水流切穿岩石，暴露出丰富的铁矿层。

英文原文: Agalmon sits in a valley in the Dryatic Mountains. The soil here is rich for a highland region, and the villagers have learned how to use terracing and other techniques to coax grain out of the mountain soil.
中文译文: 阿加尔蒙位于德亚提克山脉的一个山谷中。对于高地地区而言，这里的土壤颇为肥沃，村民们学会了利用梯田等技术从山地土壤中获取谷物。

英文原文: Epicrotea, the 'All-Conquering' was named after the eccentric long-haired military genius Sarapios, who crushed a Battanian host and secured the lands west of Lake Laconis for the Empire. Its towers, overlooking the Miron river, have frequently been a northerner's first glimpse of the awesome and terrible force to their south.
中文译文: 埃皮克罗提亚，意为“征服一切”，以古怪的长发军事天才萨拉皮奥斯命名，他击溃了巴旦尼亚大军，为帝国稳固了拉科尼斯湖以西的土地。其塔楼俯瞰米隆河，常常是北方人首次目睹南方那令人敬畏与恐惧的力量之处。

英文原文: Marathea lies in a valley in the Dryatic Mountains, overlooking Lake Laconis. The villagers are skilled miners, and the red streaks running down the nearby cliffsides are evidence of rich iron ores.
中文译文: 马拉西亚位于德亚提克山脉的一个山谷中，俯瞰拉科尼斯湖。村民们是熟练的矿工，附近悬崖上流淌的红色条纹证明了丰富的铁矿存在。

英文原文: Stathymos lies by a tributary of the Miron River, north of the Dryatic Mountains. The nearby hills have several hot springs, whose faint whiff of sulfur has made Stathymos one of several villages in the Empire who claim to have once been plagued by dragons who were eventually slain by bold cataphracts.
中文译文: 斯塔西莫斯位于德亚提克山脉以北的米隆河支流旁。附近的山丘有几处温泉，淡淡的硫磺气味使斯塔西莫斯成为帝国内数个声称曾受龙患、最终被英勇的铁甲骑兵斩杀龙的村庄之一。

英文原文: Gymos is a quiet fishing village in the Diathmic Bay, on the far west of Lake Laconis. Villagers harvest pike from the bay's deep waters.
中文译文: 吉莫斯是拉科尼斯湖最西端迪亚斯玛湾的一个宁静渔村。村民们从海湾深水中捕捞梭鱼。

英文原文: Dyopalis lies in a valley in the Lesser Dryatic Mountains. Despite the cold winters, the region's fertile soils make this district one of the breadbaskets of the northern Empire.
中文译文: 迪奥帕利斯位于小德亚提克山脉的一个山谷中。尽管冬季寒冷，该地区肥沃的土壤使这片区域成为北帝国的粮仓之一。

英文原文: Diathma, the city of the Divine Decree, was erected by an imperial general on the ruins of a Dryatic shrine overlooking Lake Laconis. It was the last stand of the Dryatics, the northernmost of the Palaic peoples, who rebelled against the Empire and were overcome. According to legend they were pushed back into the shallows of the lake and fought until they were exhausted and the bloody waters engulfed them.
中文译文: 迪亚斯玛，神谕之城，由一位帝国将军在俯瞰拉科尼斯湖的德亚提克神殿废墟上建立。这里是德亚提克人——帕莱克民族中最北的一支——的最后据点，他们反抗帝国并被击败。据传说，他们被逼退至湖的浅滩，战斗至力竭，血水将他们吞没。

英文原文: Alosea lies on the southern shores of Lake Laconis. The folk of this region live well off the pike and sturgeon of the lake.
中文译文: 阿洛塞亚位于拉科尼斯湖南岸。此地的居民依靠湖中的梭鱼和鲟鱼过着富足的生活。

英文原文: Jeracos sits in meadowland in the northern piedmont of the Dryatic Mountains. The slopes of the nearby hills are pierced with mineshafts. Despite centuries of use, they still yield ores from which silver can be extracted through various cunning techniques.
中文译文: 杰拉科斯位于德亚提克山脉北麓的草地上。附近的山坡上矿井密布。尽管开采了数百年，这些矿井仍能产出矿石，通过多种巧妙工艺提炼出白银。

英文原文: Masangara sits on the banks of the Nevys, a cold, slow-moving river that empties into Lake Laconis. Cattle are raised here in former marshy woodlands, now drained and turned into meadows.
中文译文: 马桑加拉位于内维斯河畔，这条冰冷缓慢的河流注入拉科尼斯湖。这里过去是沼泽林地，如今已被排干变为牧场，用于饲养牛群。

英文原文: Saneopa sits on a low pass leading from the Nevys valley to the north to the pool of the Ophthys. The ancient Palaic peoples of this region, like the Battanians, treated lakes as sacred sites, and the Ophthys in particular was said to be a gateway to another world. The hustle and bustle of Saneopa, one of the main trading centers of inland Calradia, stands in sharp contrast to the dark and silent waters below.
中文译文: 萨内奥帕坐落于一条低矮隘口上，该隘口从北方的内维斯山谷通往奥菲斯湖。此地的古老帕莱克民族，如同巴坦尼亚人一般，视湖泊为圣地，尤其传说奥菲斯湖是通往另一个世界的大门。作为卡拉迪亚内陆主要贸易中心之一的萨内奥帕，其喧嚣繁华与下方幽暗寂静的湖水形成鲜明对比。

英文原文: Enoisa sits by the cold, slow-moving River Nevys as it makes its way towards Lake Laconis. Wheat grows here, fed by the ample rains of the north.
中文译文: 埃诺伊萨依偎在冰冷而缓慢流淌的内维斯河畔，该河蜿蜒流向拉科尼斯湖。得益于北方充沛的雨水，此地小麦生长繁茂。

英文原文: Aeoria sits in the fertile Vale of the Aris, where the river winds between parallel granite ridges. The dialect here is laced with words from Palaic, the language spoken in the center of the continent before the Empire came.
中文译文: 艾奥里亚坐落在肥沃的阿里斯山谷中，河流在平行的花岗岩山脊间蜿蜒。此地的方言中夹杂着帕莱克语的词汇——那是帝国到来之前大陆中部所使用的语言。

英文原文: Crios sits besides the River Aris as it descends into the Ophthys, the dark pool that lies at the foot of Mount Tarnea. Shrines built by the ancient Palaic people, who believed the lake was a gateway to another worgendsd, can be found along its shores.
中文译文: 克里奥斯位于阿里斯河畔，该河在此流入奥菲斯湖——塔尔尼亚山脚下的一片幽暗水域。古代帕莱克人建造的神龛沿湖岸分布，他们相信这座湖是通往另一个世界的大门。

英文原文: The city of Argoron sits where the cold, slow moving Nevys River meets Lake Laconis. This was once the land of the Laconians, who saw the Empire as a protector against the more warlike Palaic peoples. They welcomed and intermarried with imperial settlers and their chiefs joined the Senate, and their old language and customs faded away.
中文译文: 阿戈隆城坐落于冰冷缓慢的内维斯河与拉科尼斯湖交汇处。这里曾是拉科尼亚人的土地，他们将帝国视为对抗好战的帕莱克民族的保护者。他们欢迎帝国定居者并与之通婚，其首领加入了元老院，古老的语言与习俗逐渐消亡。

英文原文: Potamis sits at the edge of the Temea Valley, near the foothills of the Dryatic Mountains. The people of these parts have raised cattle since pre-Calradic times, and its milk and butter are sought out by traders and travellers passing through the region.
中文译文: 波塔米斯位于特梅亚山谷边缘，靠近德里亚提克山脉的山麓。自前卡拉迪亚时代以来，此地居民便以养牛为生，其牛奶与黄油备受途经此地的商旅青睐。

英文原文: Alatys lies on the flat southern end of Lake Laconis, on delta lowlands formed by the River Nevys. The waters of the lake, which are very shallow and muddy here, are well-stocked with flounder and crayfish, while trout can be fished in the Nevys upstream.
中文译文: 阿拉蒂斯位于拉科尼斯湖平坦的南端，坐落在内维斯河形成的三角洲低地上。此处湖水极浅且浑浊，盛产比目鱼和螯虾，而鳟鱼则可在内维斯河上游垂钓。

英文原文: Nortanisa is a fishing village on the western shores of Lake Laconis. Villagers place nets and cages in the shallow edges of the lake and the nearby estuary of the Caracasos to catch roach, bream, perch, and crayfish.
中文译文: 诺塔尼萨是拉科尼斯湖西岸的一个渔村。村民们在浅湖边缘及附近的卡拉卡索斯河口放置渔网和笼子，捕捞蟑螂、欧鳊、鲈鱼和螯虾。

英文原文: Hetania sits beneath a low ridge in the Lesser Dryatic Mountains. As in many villages around here, the foresters of this region still speak the Dryatic languages of the tribes who lived here before the Empire came.
中文译文: 赫塔尼亚坐落于小德里亚提克山脉的一道低矮山脊之下。与周围许多村庄一样，此地的林务员仍使用帝国到来前居住于此的部落的德里亚提克语。

英文原文: Myzea gives its name to the Myzead, the mountainous highlands of the eastern Empire. This region is the old stomping grounds of the senator Litos, who brought the Palaic tribes here into the Empire relatively peacefully by offering them protection against raiders from the steppes. When he fell afoul of his rivals in the Senate, however, he tried to organize them into a rival 'barbarian' republic modelled on the ancient Calradian version. The empire snuffed it out, but the people here, now thoroughly Calradicized, still harbor an attachment to the old republican system and its partisans.
中文译文: 迈泽亚之名源自迈泽阿德——帝国东部的崎岖高地。此区域是元老利托斯的旧日领地，他通过为帕莱克部落提供对抗草原掠夺者的保护，相对和平地将他们纳入帝国。然而，当他与元老院中的对手交恶时，他试图将他们组织成一个以古代卡拉迪亚共和国为蓝本的敌对“蛮族”共和国。帝国镇压了这场运动，但此地的人民——如今已彻底卡拉迪亚化——仍对旧共和制度及其拥护者怀有依恋。

英文原文: Vealos sits in a thickly wooded valley in the eastern end of the Myzead, the rugged highlands of the eastern Empire. Retired imperial officers have founded stud farms here, grazing their horses on the rich pasture on the slopes of the heights.
中文译文: 维阿洛斯坐落在迈泽阿德东部一片林木茂密的山谷中，迈泽阿德是帝国东部的崎岖高地。退役的帝国军官在此建立了种马场，让马匹在高地山坡的丰美牧场上放牧。

英文原文: Orthra, a relatively recent settlement, was named by an imperial captain for its fine southward views over the Myzead, the mountainous highlands of the eastern empire. It was founded as a stud farm to breed imperial horses with steppe stock from the east, and continues to provide horses for the imperial cavalry.
中文译文: 奥斯拉是一个相对较新的定居点，由一位帝国队长以其向南俯瞰迈泽阿德（帝国东部的崎岖高地）的优美景色而命名。它最初是作为种马场建立的，旨在用东方的草原马种培育帝国战马，至今仍为帝国骑兵提供马匹。

英文原文: Themys lies under a ridge of limestone crags in the Myzead, the mountainous highlands of the eastern Empire. The grasses on the rolling downs are well-suited to the raising of sheep.
中文译文: 塞米斯位于迈泽阿德（帝国东部的崎岖高地）一道石灰岩峭壁的山脊之下。起伏丘陵上的草场非常适合养羊。

英文原文: Samatha sits upon a sloping ridge in the highlands of the Myzead. The hillsides have been terraced to allow the growing of mulberry bushes. The leaves are harvested, placed in vats and fed to silkworms.
中文译文: 萨马萨坐落在迈泽阿德高地的一道倾斜山脊上。山坡被开垦成梯田以种植桑树。桑叶被采摘后放入槽中喂养蚕虫。

英文原文: Amprela is the foremost town of the northeastern Empire. Watchmen stand atop its towers, looking out for beacons from the frontier fortresses to the north and east that might signal the approach of enemies. Horse archers recruited from the rough Eleftheroi freemen drink in the barracks, waiting for the order to ride out against Khuzait steppe-riders, Sturgian woodsmen or Nord longships coming down the Caracasos River, while the cataphracts drill in the courtyard of the keep and ensure their horses are in fighting trim should the archon need to assemble an army to deal with any larger threat.
中文译文: 安普雷拉是帝国东北部最重要的城镇。守望者立于塔楼之上，眺望北方和东方边境堡垒的烽火，那可能预示着敌人的逼近。从粗犷的埃莱夫塞罗伊自由民中招募的骑射手在营房中饮酒，等待命令出击对抗库赛特草原骑手、斯特吉亚林务员或沿卡拉卡索斯河而下的诺德长船；而铁甲圣骑兵则在城堡庭院中操练，确保他们的战马处于战斗状态，以备执政官需要集结军队应对更大威胁。

英文原文: Avasinton sits in a valley of the Myzead, the mountainous highlands of the eastern Empire. Villagers have cleared away the thickets and thornbrush of this region, including the pungent wormwood after which the village is named, to allow the growing of mulberry bushes, the leaves of which are harvested and fed to silkworms.
中文译文: 阿瓦辛顿坐落在迈泽阿德（帝国东部的崎岖高地）的一座山谷中。村民们清除了此地的灌木丛和荆棘，包括以其命名的刺鼻苦艾，以便种植桑树，桑叶被采摘后用于喂养蚕虫。

英文原文: Boreagora lies where the estuary of the Caracasos River reaches Lake Laconis. The lowlands here are suited to the growing of flax.
中文译文: 博雷阿戈拉位于卡拉卡索斯河河口与拉科尼斯湖交汇之处。此地的低地适合种植亚麻。

英文原文: Garontor gives its name to the straits that join the Gulf of Charas and the inland Perassic Sea, the Gates of Garontor. Coastal meadows, where tall grasses tolerant of the ocean salt-spray thrive, support the rearing of fine horses.
中文译文: 加隆托尔之名赋予了连接查拉斯湾与内陆佩拉西克海的狭窄海峡——加隆托尔之门。沿海草甸上，耐盐雾的高草繁茂生长，滋养着优良马匹的培育。

英文原文: Thorios sits in the Temea Valley in the heart of Calradia. Ample rainfall, rich soil and the relatively warm inland climate ensure that the grain fields of this district feed much of the central empire.
中文译文: 索里奥斯坐落于卡拉迪亚腹地的特梅亚山谷。充沛的降雨、肥沃的土壤以及相对温暖的内陆气候，确保了这片区域的麦田供养着帝国中部的大部分地区。

英文原文: Bergum sits in the Temea Valley in the heart of Calradia. Rich soil, ample rainfall and the warm inland climate ensure that the grain fields of this district feed much of the central empire.
中文译文: 贝格姆位于卡拉迪亚腹地的特梅亚山谷。肥沃的土壤、充沛的降雨和温暖的内陆气候，使得这片区域的麦田供养着帝国中部的大部分地区。

英文原文: Onica lies in the Iltaric Hills, north of the Perassic Sea. The slopes of this district are terraced with vineyards, producing a dry wine popular throughout the Empire.
中文译文: 奥尼卡位于佩拉西克海以北的伊尔塔里克丘陵。这片区域的山坡上梯田遍布，种植着葡萄，酿造的干型葡萄酒在整个帝国广受欢迎。

英文原文: Thractorae sits on the hills above a small bay that feeds into the larger Gulf of Charas. Oaks grow thick around the streams running down to the sea, allowing hogs to forage acorns and roots.
中文译文: 斯拉克托雷坐落在流入查拉斯湾的一个小海湾上方的山丘上。橡树在流向大海的溪流周围茂密生长，使得猪群能够觅食橡果和树根。

英文原文: Veron lies on the Gavys River, the westernmost tributary of the Aminys. The hills above the town are lined with vineyards which thrive in the well-drained, sandy soil.
中文译文: 维隆位于加维斯河畔，这是阿米尼斯河最西端的支流。城镇上方的山丘上遍布葡萄园，它们在排水良好的沙质土壤中茁壮成长。

英文原文: Goleryn sits in the warm valley of the Gavys, where the river brushes by an outcrop of the Epiric Hills. Sheep-raising is a way of life in these parts, as is sheep-rustling.
中文译文: 戈勒林坐落在温暖的加维斯河谷，河流擦过埃皮里克丘陵的一处露头。养羊是此地的生活方式，偷羊亦然。

英文原文: Hertogea lies in the shadow of Mount Erithrys. Villagers grow olives on the slopes leading up the mountain. This region is home to a number of hot springs as well as caves that smell of the vapors of the earth. One of them - it is known not which - was home to a Palaic oracle who delivered a series of prophecies about the rise and eventual fall of the Empire. Imperial scholars with a mystical bent hotly debate which of the cryptic verses are authentic, and which ones are later inventions.
中文译文: 赫托盖亚位于埃里瑟里斯山的阴影之下。村民们在上山的山坡上种植橄榄。这片区域拥有众多温泉，以及散发着大地气息的洞穴。其中一处——无人知晓是哪一处——曾是一位帕莱克先知的家，他预言了帝国的兴起与最终衰落。具有神秘倾向的帝国学者们激烈争论哪些隐晦的诗句是真实的，哪些是后人的杜撰。

英文原文: Oristocorys lies on a peninsula west of the Bay of Ortysia. The village sits high on a slope to avoid both mosquitos from coastal marshes and raiding corsairs from the south, but the villagers make their way each morning to the sheltered covers where they keep their boats and set out to catch tuna, swordfish and other large species that migrate through the bay.
中文译文: 奥里斯托科里斯位于奥提西亚湾以西的一个半岛上。村庄高踞山坡，以避开沿海沼泽的蚊虫和南方来袭的海盗，但村民们每天早晨都会前往隐蔽的海湾，那里停泊着他们的船只，他们出海捕捞金枪鱼、剑鱼以及其他洄游经过海湾的大型鱼类。

英文原文: Elvania overlooks the estuary of the Aminys River. Hot southern afternoons and sea-cooled mornings make this good country for wine grapes.
中文译文: 埃尔瓦尼亚俯瞰着阿米尼斯河的入海口。炎热的南方午后与海风清凉的清晨，使这里成为种植酿酒葡萄的佳地。

英文原文: Gersegos sits on a northern spur of the great basalt plutonic slab of Mount Erithrys. Villagers have sunk mines into the cliffs that overlook the village, extracting silver and other precious metals.
中文译文: 格塞戈斯坐落在埃里瑟里斯山巨大玄武岩岩体的北支脉上。村民们在俯瞰村庄的悬崖上开凿矿井，开采白银和其他贵金属。

英文原文: Lageta was infamously acquired by the Empire by an act of treachery. A rogue imperial mercenary murdered the Palaic chieftain who employed him, took the citadel, invited in imperial colonists, then bequeathed the town to the Empire. The Senate disavowed his action but accepted the bequest. The district is now thoroughly Calradicized, but the Battanians to the north remember, and whenever an imperial envoy speaks of the sanctity of treaties and pacts, they just nod to each other and say, 'Lageta.'
中文译文: 拉格塔因帝国通过背信弃义的手段获得而臭名昭著。一名叛变的帝国雇佣兵杀害了雇佣他的帕莱克酋长，夺取了城堡，邀请帝国殖民者进入，然后将城镇遗赠给帝国。元老院谴责了他的行为，但接受了遗赠。如今这片区域已彻底卡拉迪亚化，但北方的巴坦尼亚人铭记着，每当帝国使节谈及条约与盟约的神圣性时，他们只是相互点头，说道：“拉格塔。”

英文原文: Primessos lies in the wooded upper reaches of the Aminys valley. Pigs, fed on the acorns of the southern oak trees, thrive in this climate.
中文译文: 普里梅索斯位于阿米尼斯河谷林木茂密的上游地区。以南方橡树的橡果为食的猪群在此气候中茁壮成长。

英文原文: Montos sits near the headwaters of the Aminys River, close to the Battanian frontier. The farmers here keep a watchful eye when tending their hillside fields, always looking for raiding parties coming westward from the valley of the Miron River to their east.
中文译文: 蒙托斯靠近阿米尼斯河源头，毗邻巴坦尼亚边境。这里的农民在照料山坡田地时始终保持警惕，时刻提防从东边米隆河谷向西而来的劫掠队伍。

英文原文: Nideon sits on a low-lying spit of land where the Gavys, Aminys and Zeos rivers come together. Wine grapes grow on the gentle slopes rising up from the river valley.
中文译文: 尼迪昂坐落在加维斯河、阿米尼斯河与泽奥斯河交汇处的一片低洼沙嘴上。酿酒葡萄生长在河谷缓缓升起的山坡上。

英文原文: Zeonica sits by the seaside at the base of the Iltaric Hills, a land of wine, grain and horses. It was named for the Lady Zeona, one of the few women to rule the Empire - admittedly as regent for her son rather than Empress Regnant. It is known for the Zeonic Wind, a gale that blows northward across the Perassic Sea every summer carrying the burning heat of the Nahasa desert to the imperial south. In other seasons the weather here is mild, a weather pattern that has provided many a poet with a metaphor for the transience of passion.
中文译文: 泽奥尼卡位于伊尔塔里克丘陵脚下的海滨，是一片盛产葡萄酒、谷物和马匹的土地。它以贵妇泽奥娜命名，她是少数统治过帝国的女性之一——诚然，是作为儿子的摄政而非女皇。此地以“泽奥尼克温德号”闻名，每年夏季，这股强风向北吹过佩拉西克海，将纳哈萨沙漠的灼热带到帝国南方。在其他季节，这里气候温和，这种天气模式为众多诗人提供了比喻激情易逝的素材。

英文原文: Tarcutis sits on the shores of the Perassic Sea. The sandy soil and morning mists, channeled by offshore islands, allows the growing of grapes overlooking the beaches.
中文译文: 塔库提斯坐落在佩拉西克海之滨。沙质土壤和由近海岛屿引导的晨雾，使得海滩上方的山坡能够种植葡萄。

英文原文: Neocorys, the new village, sits in gently sloping land in the Temea Valley cleared only recently of forest. The local landowners have established stud farms, supplying horses to the imperial cavalry.
中文译文: 尼奥科里斯，意为新村，坐落在特梅亚山谷平缓起伏的土地上，这里不久前才从森林中开垦出来。当地地主建立了种马场，为帝国骑兵供应马匹。

英文原文: Alsasos sits on a shelf of land at the west end of the Temea, a broad valley north of the Perassic sea. Though the climate is relatively dry here, artesian wells allow farmers to grow bountiful crops of wheat.
中文译文: 阿尔萨索斯位于特梅亚西端的一片台地上，特梅亚是佩拉西克海以北的宽阔山谷。尽管此地气候相对干燥，但自流井使农民能够种植丰产的小麦。

英文原文: Zeocorys lies at the foot of a low escarpment that bisects the Temea Valley. Though the climate is relatively dry here, artesian wells allow farmers to grow bountiful crops of wheat.
中文译文: 泽奥科里斯位于分隔特梅亚山谷的一道低矮悬崖脚下。尽管此地气候相对干燥，但自流井使农民能够种植丰产的小麦。

英文原文: Every realm has a city whose inhabitants are mocked for their provincial unsophistication, and for the Empire that city was Jalmarys. One of the first imperial colonies established inland of the Perassic Sea, it served the Empire as a base to subdue the western Palaic tribes. Paravenos was happy to accept the plunder and slaves sent to them from its garrison, but snickered at them behind their backs. That changed when the Jalmaryan general Carsos staged a military coup. When a senator, stymied by his thick accent, nervously asked him to repeat a decree, he unsheathed his sword and said, 'Do you understand this?'
中文译文: 每个王国都有一座被嘲为乡巴佬的城市，对帝国而言，那座城便是贾尔马里斯。作为帝国在佩拉西克海内陆最早建立的殖民地之一，它曾是帝国镇压西部帕莱克部落的基地。帕拉维诺斯人欣然接受驻军送来的战利品与奴隶，却在背后嗤笑。直到贾尔马里斯将军卡尔索斯发动军事政变，一切才改变。当一位议员被他浓重的口音所困，紧张地请他重复法令时，他拔剑出鞘，说道：“这个你懂吗？”

英文原文: Gamardan sits at a key gap in the Epiric Hills, on the route between Charas and the imperial cities of the Aminys valley. The wines of this region are unusually sweet and strong, and valued throughout the Empire.
中文译文: 加马尔丹坐落在埃皮里克丘陵的一处关键隘口，位于查拉斯与阿米尼斯河谷的帝国城市之间的路线上。此地的葡萄酒异常甘甜醇厚，在整个帝国备受珍视。

英文原文: Aegosca sits at the head of the Temea, a broad valley in southern Calradia. Though originally named for the goats that used to roam this region, the landowners have turned over the pastures to the raising of far more profitable horses.
中文译文: 埃戈斯卡位于卡拉迪亚南部宽阔的特梅亚河谷源头。尽管最初以曾在此地漫游的山羊命名，但地主们已将牧场转为饲养利润更高的马匹。

英文原文: Dradios sits near a bend in the Zeos River. Here the meandering waters have left a rich deposit of silt that sustains one of the breadbaskets of the Empire.
中文译文: 德拉迪奥斯坐落在泽奥斯河的一处河湾附近。蜿蜒的河水在此留下了肥沃的淤泥，滋养着帝国粮仓之一。

英文原文: Ortysia, named for the quails who inhabit the nearby coastal marshes, was the first Calradian colony on the shores of the Perassic Sea. In its early centuries, it fell under the authority of the Kannic city-state of Quyaz, but the Empire's expansion soon united the colony with its mother republic. For centuries, sailors coming from the pirate-infested islands of the Gulf of Charas gave thanks when they saw its towers looming through the haze of the western Perassic, signalling a return to safe imperial-patrolled waters.
中文译文: 奥提西亚以栖息在附近沿海沼泽的鹌鹑命名，是帝国在佩拉西克海沿岸的第一个卡拉迪亚殖民地。在其早期几个世纪，它曾受卡尼克城邦库亚兹管辖，但帝国的扩张很快将殖民地与其母邦共和国重新统一。数个世纪以来，来自查拉斯湾海盗出没岛屿的水手们，当看到它的塔楼从西佩拉西克海雾中隐约浮现时，便感恩戴德，因为这标志着回到了帝国巡逻的安全水域。

英文原文: Arpotis lies close to where the Zeos and Gavys rivers join the Aminys and together reach the sea. This well-watered plain is one of the breadbaskets of the Empire.
中文译文: 阿尔波蒂斯位于泽奥斯河与加维斯河汇入阿米尼斯河并一同入海之处。这片水源充足的平原是帝国粮仓之一。

英文原文: Lysia sits on the promontory above the Gates of Garontor, the straits that link the Gulf of Charas with the Perassic. Salt is farmed from evaporation pans on the broad beaches that look out onto the gulf.
中文译文: 利西亚坐落在加隆托尔海峡之上的海岬，该海峡连接查拉斯湾与佩拉西克海。在俯瞰海湾的宽阔海滩上，人们利用蒸发池制盐。

英文原文: Vinela on the landward side of the Epiric Hills, facing the Gavys River. Early morning sea-mists that come up the Gavys valley from the Perassic, together with the hot sun of midday, allow the growing of wine grapes.
中文译文: 维内拉位于埃皮里克丘陵靠陆一侧，面向加维斯河。清晨从佩拉西克海沿加维斯河谷升起的海雾，加上正午的烈日，使得葡萄得以生长。

英文原文: Garengolia lies near a rich vein of silver in the eastern end of the Epiric Hills. The Empire have minted its denars from these mines for centuries.
中文译文: 加伦戈利亚靠近埃皮里克丘陵东端一处丰富的银矿脉。帝国数百年来一直用这些矿藏铸造第纳尔。

英文原文: The Palaic chieftain of Amitatys had a particularly grim reputation, a tyrant who slaughtered innocents to appease the gods of the underworld in the hope they would grant him immortality. Inevitably, he fell into conflict with the Empire, and during the siege an earthquake crumbled his walls and allowed imperial troops into the city. More died in the ensuing sack than the tyrant had ever killed, but it was war, and the outcome - attributed to the wrath of Heaven - was a staple of imperial propaganda as they expanded their foothold in the region.
中文译文: 阿米塔提斯的帕莱克酋长有着尤为凶残的名声，他是个暴君，屠杀无辜以取悦冥界诸神，祈求获得永生。他不可避免地与帝国发生冲突，围城期间一场地震震塌了城墙，帝国军队得以入城。随后的大屠杀中死亡人数远超暴君一生所杀，但这是战争，其结局——归因于天怒——成为帝国在该地区扩张立足点时的宣传利器。

英文原文: Elipa sits on the heights overlooking the Plain of Ichala, a sparsely populated ancient lake bed. The plain itself is dry and the soil saline, and is mostly empty grass and scrubland, but the heights above are fertile and receives ample rains.
中文译文: 埃利帕坐落在俯瞰伊查拉平原的高地上，那是一片人烟稀少的古湖床。平原本身干燥，土壤盐碱化，多为空旷的草地与灌木丛，但高地之上却土地肥沃，雨水充沛。

英文原文: Phasos sits amid rolling chalk uplands on the edge of the Temea Valley. The rich grass of the down is some of the best horse-breeding land in the Empire.
中文译文: 法索斯坐落在特梅亚河谷边缘起伏的白垩高地上。丘陵上丰茂的牧草是帝国最好的养马地之一。

英文原文: Rhotae is a former Palaic hill-fort in the shadow of the basalt slab of Mount Erithrys, once known as Rotash. It fell to the Empire shortly afer Lageta. The Palaic chief here, when he heard of what happened to his neighbor, slaughtered a dozen imperial citizens under his protection. The eccentric long-haired general Sarapios led the punitive column that took Rotash and burned it to the ground. 'Lageta we took treacherously but with little bloodshed. Rotash we took honorably, with great bloodshed,' he supposedly said afterwards. 'Both will be remembered as a stain on our name. The victor always takes the blame, but keeps the gold.'
中文译文: 罗泰是埃里瑟里斯山玄武岩板阴影下的一座前帕莱克山堡，曾名罗塔什。它在拉格塔陷落后不久落入帝国之手。此地的帕莱克酋长听闻邻邦遭遇后，屠杀了受其保护的十几名帝国公民。古怪的长发将军萨拉皮奥斯率领惩罚纵队攻占罗塔什并将其夷为平地。“拉格塔我们以诡计夺取，但流血甚少。罗塔什我们以荣誉夺取，流血甚多，”他事后据说如此说道。“两者都将作为我们名声上的污点被铭记。胜利者总是承担罪责，但保留黄金。”

英文原文: Carphenion lies in the rolling hills east of Mount Erithrys. On summer nights the hills above the village are dotted with the fires of cattle herders, taking their animals to graze on the upland pastures.
中文译文: 卡菲尼昂位于埃里瑟里斯山以东的起伏丘陵中。夏夜，村庄上方的丘陵上点缀着牧牛人的篝火，他们赶着牲畜到高地牧场放牧。

英文原文: Vathea sits high on the slopes of the Dryatic Mountains, facing across the valley to the great basalt slab of Mount Erithrys. The villagers mine iron from shafts sunk deep into the cliffs of the mountains.
中文译文: 瓦西亚高踞于德里亚提克山脉的斜坡上，隔谷相望埃里瑟里斯山巨大的玄武岩板。村民从深凿入山崖的矿井中开采铁矿。

英文原文: Thersenion lies in the shadow of Mount Erithrys, a great basalt slab thrust up from the depths of the earth. The Gavys, Aminys amd Zeos rivers all come together in the plains beneath the mountain, making these lands one of the grain breadbaskets of the Empire.
中文译文: 瑟森尼翁位于埃里瑟里斯山的阴影下，那是一座从地底深处隆起的巨大玄武岩板。加维斯河、阿米尼斯河与泽奥斯河在山下平原交汇，使这片土地成为帝国粮仓之一。

英文原文: Leblenion sits in the rolling hills of the Temea Valley east of Mount Erithrys, a great basalt slab thrust up from the depths of the earth. This warm land is one of the grain breadbaskets of the Empire.
中文译文: 莱布莱尼昂坐落在埃里瑟里斯山以东特梅亚河谷的起伏丘陵中，埃里瑟里斯山是一座从地底深处隆起的巨大玄武岩板。这片温暖的土地是帝国粮仓之一。

英文原文: Odrysa lies near the Aserai frontier at the Calsean Gates, where the hills come down almost to the sea. The boulders here are covered with thousands of years of graffiti, in Calradic, in Nahasawi, Darshi, Kannic and a dozen other languages, from conquerors' triumphant declarations to their soldiers' homesick complaints. Though this area is dry, cedars and other trees grow in gorges in the hills, sustaining a small lumber industry.
中文译文: 奥德里萨坐落于阿塞莱边境附近的卡尔西安城门，那里山丘几乎延伸至海边。巨石上覆盖着数千年的涂鸦，用卡拉迪亚语、纳哈萨维语、达尔希语、坎尼克语以及其他十几种语言写成，既有征服者的胜利宣言，也有士兵思乡的抱怨。尽管此地干燥，雪松和其他树木仍生长在山丘的峡谷中，支撑着小规模的木材业。

英文原文: Corenia is one of the farthest flung villages of the Empire to speak the Calradic tongue. It lies in the north of the Tanaetic Plain, on the shores of Lake Tanaesis. Villagers grow grain here, keeping watch for raiders from across the frontiers.
中文译文: 科雷尼亚是帝国最偏远的讲卡拉迪亚语的村庄之一。它位于塔纳埃提克平原北部，塔纳埃西斯湖畔。村民们在这里种植谷物，同时警惕边境外的袭击者。

英文原文: Metachia lies on the southern shores of Lake Tanaesis, where the coastal plain begins to climb towards the ridges of the Myzead. This imperial colony, established in more peaceful times, is a center for mulberry bushes used in silk production.
中文译文: 梅塔基亚位于塔纳埃西斯湖南岸，沿海平原在此开始向迈泽亚德山脊攀升。这个帝国殖民地建立于更和平的年代，是用于丝绸生产的桑树种植中心。

英文原文: Melion sits in a narrow valley near the center of the Myzead, the rugged highlands in the east of the Empire. Villagers here cultivate mulberry bushes. The leaves are harvested and fed to silkworks, producing thread much valued by both refined imperial nobles for their courtwear and hard-riding nomads and frontiersmen, who wear it beneath their armor as an extra layer of protection against arrows.
中文译文: 梅利昂坐落在帝国东部崎岖高地迈泽亚德中心附近的一个狭窄山谷中。村民们在这里种植桑树。采摘的桑叶用于喂养蚕虫，生产出的丝线备受珍视——无论是帝国贵族用于宫廷服饰，还是骑马的游牧民和边疆居民，他们将其穿在铠甲下作为抵御箭矢的额外保护层。

英文原文: Sagolina sits at the foot of a narrow valley at the southern end of the Myzead, the rugged highlands of eastern Calradia. Villagers mine salt from deposits in the hills.
中文译文: 萨戈利纳位于卡拉迪亚东部崎岖高地迈泽亚德南端的一个狭窄山谷脚下。村民们从山丘的矿床中开采盐。

英文原文: Lavenia sits on the northeastern shores of the Perassic. The people of this region sustain themselves by fishing, but also by serving on merchant vessels that haul cargos across the great inland sea, the imperial fleets that protect them, or the corsairs that prey on them.
中文译文: 拉维尼亚位于佩拉西克海东北海岸。该地区的人们以捕鱼为生，同时也服务于横跨内海的商船、保护商船的帝国舰队，或劫掠商船的海盗。

英文原文: Ethemisa lies in a low valley in the Thymian Hills. This region was once barren and mostly deserted, save for the hermits and visionaries who made their homes in its limestone caves. Ethemisa was home to some of the first permanent migrants, who tapped wells on the valley floor to irrigate their fields. According to legend, they demanded that one of the holy recluses here serve as their leader and guide them to virtue. At first he refused, but Heaven sent a flock of goats to follow him everywhere, disturbing his solitude, until he at least repented.
中文译文: 埃塞米萨位于蒂米安丘陵的一个低洼山谷中。这片区域曾经荒芜且人烟稀少，只有隐士和先知在石灰岩洞穴中安家。埃塞米萨是最早一批永久移民的定居地，他们在谷底挖井灌溉田地。据传说，他们要求这里的一位圣洁隐士担任他们的领袖，引导他们走向美德。起初他拒绝了，但上天派了一群山羊到处跟随他，扰乱他的独处，直到他最终悔悟。

英文原文: Morenia sits overlooking the Sethys River. Villagers collect clay from the riverbanks for the kilns of the imperial southlands.
中文译文: 莫雷尼亚俯瞰塞西斯河。村民们从河岸采集黏土，供应帝国南部地区的窑炉。

英文原文: Sestadaim sits on the western side of the great Ornian Rock, looking over the dry Plain of Ichala. Some say the vultures for which the rock is named once gathered here to feast on the bodies of those who died working the local salt mines.
中文译文: 塞斯塔代姆位于巨大的奥尔尼安岩西侧，俯瞰干燥的伊查拉平原。有人说，以岩石命名的秃鹫曾聚集于此，享用那些死于当地盐矿开采者的尸体。

英文原文: Jogurys lies on the flat western shores of Lake Tanaesis. This land was originally inhabited by nomadic tribes and has come under cultivation only recently. Imperial colonists have made intensive efforts to grow mullberry bushes here to raise silkworms, a lucrative product in this region.
中文译文: 约古里斯位于塔纳埃西斯湖平坦的西岸。这片土地最初由游牧部落居住，直到最近才被开垦。帝国殖民者在此大力种植桑树以养蚕，这是该地区利润丰厚的产品。

英文原文: Chanopsis sits near the headwaters of the Sethys River. Wheat grows well on the sun-baked slopes of the valley.
中文译文: 查诺普西斯位于塞西斯河源头附近。小麦在阳光炙烤的山坡上生长良好。

英文原文: The frontier city of Danustica has long defended the eastern reaches of the Empire, holding the line against incursions by the Aserai and various Darshi shahs and padishahs further to the east. Many an imperial legionary stationed here has scribbled graffiti on its walls cursing the city's sweltering summers, brackish water and mosquitos, but the inhabitants are proud of their role as border defenders and mock the soldiers wilting in the heat around them.
中文译文: 边境城市达努斯蒂卡长期保卫着帝国的东部疆域，抵御阿塞莱以及更东方的各种达尔希沙阿和帕迪沙阿的入侵。许多驻扎在此的帝国军团士兵在城墙上涂鸦，诅咒这里闷热的夏天、苦涩的水和蚊子，但居民们为自己作为边境守卫者的角色感到自豪，并嘲笑那些在酷热中萎靡不振的士兵。

英文原文: Caira sits in the humid plains east of Danustica. Here, the sea air of the Perassic is trapped by the Calsean Hills, creating a climate shunned by many other Calradians. For those used to the heat, however, the rich soils provide a good living, and the villagers are proud of their role guarding the frontier with the Aserai.
中文译文: 凯拉位于达努斯蒂卡以东的潮湿平原上。在这里，佩拉西克海的海风被卡尔西安丘陵阻挡，形成了许多其他卡拉迪亚人避之不及的气候。然而，对于习惯炎热的人来说，肥沃的土壤提供了良好的生计，村民们为自己守卫阿塞莱边境的角色感到自豪。

英文原文: Polisia sits on the eastern end of the Thymian Hills. Villagers harvest salt from pans in a shallow inlet to their south, near the town of Danustica. They prefer to live up in the hills, however, away from the thick and fetid air and the mosquitos of the coast.
中文译文: 波利西亚位于蒂米安丘陵的东端。村民们在达努斯蒂卡镇附近南边的一个浅水湾中从盐田采盐。然而，他们更喜欢住在山上，远离海岸浓重污浊的空气和蚊子。

英文原文: Tegresos sits amid woodland on the eastern end of the Thymian Hills, close to the Aserai and Khuzait domains. Originally it was settled in defiance of imperial authority by the bands of fugitives and adventurers known as the Eleftheroi, but the archons of Danustica eventually awarded this settlement a proper charter in exchange for helping to guard the frontiers.
中文译文: 泰格雷索斯位于蒂米安丘陵东端的林地中，靠近阿塞莱和库赛特的领地。最初，它是由被称为埃莱夫塞罗伊的逃亡者和冒险者团伙无视帝国权威而建立的，但达努斯蒂卡的执政官最终授予了这个定居点正式的宪章，以换取其帮助守卫边境。

英文原文: The frontier village of Erebulos sits in woodland beneath the Calsean Hills. This village was settled by Eleftheroi, though it has since been integrated into the archonate of Danustica. Villagers scoop clay from creek-bottoms here, and keep watch for Aserai and Khuzait raiders trying to infliltrate the imperial frontiers through the woods.
中文译文: 边境村庄埃雷布洛斯位于卡尔西安丘陵下的林地中。这个村庄最初由埃莱夫塞罗伊建立，但后来被并入达努斯蒂卡执政区。村民们从小溪底部舀取黏土，并警惕阿塞莱和库赛特的袭击者试图通过树林渗透帝国边境。

英文原文: Vostrum was founded by the Calradian mercenary Archacos, one of a number of rogue commanders who broke away from the Empire during its expansion. Unlike some, who tried to reproduce the Empire's republican heritage, he chose to rule as a god-king. His dynasty lasted for generations, until imperial troops overran it and smashed every haloed statue of Archacos and his successors into rubble. Even today, western and northern imperial senators accuse families from this region of being closet monarchists.
中文译文: 沃斯特鲁姆由卡拉迪亚雇佣兵阿卡科斯建立，他是帝国扩张时期脱离帝国的众多叛军指挥官之一。与一些试图重现帝国共和传统的人不同，他选择以神王的身份统治。他的王朝延续了数代，直到帝国军队将其摧毁，并将阿卡科斯及其继任者的每一尊带光环的雕像砸成碎石。直至今日，西部和北帝国的元老们仍指责来自这一地区的家族是暗藏的君主主义者。

英文原文: The village of Atphynia lies in a valley in the Thymian Hills. The rocks here are speckled with sea-shells, while salt deposits lie in the valley, and villagers claim that this was once a bay of the sea uplifted by some great subterranean force.
中文译文: 阿特菲尼亚村坐落在提米安山丘的一个山谷中。这里的岩石上点缀着海贝壳，而山谷中则有盐矿沉积，村民们声称这里曾是一个海湾，被某种巨大的地下力量抬升而成。

英文原文: Gorcorys sits on the Gorea, the southernmost tributary of the Sethys River. The dark, well-watered soil here makes this region an imperial breadbasket.
中文译文: 戈尔科里斯坐落在戈雷亚河畔，这是塞西斯河最南端的支流。这里黑暗而水分充足的土壤使该地区成为帝国的粮仓。

英文原文: Avalyps sits on the narrow but fertile shelf of land between the Perassic Sea and the Thymian Hills. The dark, well-watered soil here makes this region an imperial breadbasket.
中文译文: 阿瓦利普斯坐落在佩拉西海与提米安山丘之间狭窄但肥沃的陆架上。这里黑暗而水分充足的土壤使该地区成为帝国的粮仓。

英文原文: Alision lies in a nook of the Thymian Hills, north of the Perassic Sea. The rocks of this basin are lined with seashells, a long source of fascination to imperial philosophers who debate whether the earth experienced periodic inundations or if different portions are raised and lowered over time.
中文译文: 阿利西翁位于佩拉西海以北的提米安山丘的一个角落。这个盆地的岩石上排列着贝壳，长期以来一直是帝国哲学家们着迷的源泉，他们争论地球是经历了周期性的洪水，还是不同部分随着时间的推移而升降。

英文原文: The town of Poros sits near the northern shores of the Perassic Sea. The warm rolling hills of these parts are well suited to the raising of sheep and horses. The herdsmen have a reputation for being proud and violent, but the nobles of the Leonipardes clan who rule this land are careful to respect their autonomy. Livestock rustling and blood feuds are common here, and it is said that the Leonipardes will never interfere unless invited by both parties, but when they do, their judgments will be obeyed by all.
中文译文: 波罗斯镇坐落在佩拉西海北部海岸附近。这里温暖的起伏丘陵非常适合养羊和马。牧民们以骄傲和暴力著称，但统治这片土地的莱奥尼帕德斯家族的贵族们小心翼翼地尊重他们的自治权。牲畜盗窃和血仇在这里很常见，据说莱奥尼帕德斯家族除非受到双方邀请，否则从不干涉，但一旦他们介入，他们的裁决将得到所有人的服从。

英文原文: Canoros sits in the plains north of the Perassic Sea. This land has been good horse country since even before the Empire came, furnishing the steeds of the Lycarean riders who routed several Calradian armies before their final defeat and absorption into the imperial cavalry.
中文译文: 卡诺罗斯坐落在佩拉西海以北的平原上。即使在帝国到来之前，这片土地就是优良的马场，为莱卡雷安骑手提供战马，这些骑手在最终失败并被并入帝国骑兵之前，曾击溃了数支卡拉迪亚军队。

英文原文: Tevea sits by the banks of the River Sethys, south of the Ornian Rock. This great outcrop is named for the vultures who perch on its crags to keep watch on the hazy plains below. The low-lying riverlands are used by the villagers to grow flax.
中文译文: 特维亚坐落在塞西斯河畔，奥尔尼安岩以南。这块巨大的突出岩石以栖息在其峭壁上、俯瞰下方朦胧平原的基普沃奇号命名。低洼的河岸地被村民们用来种植亚麻。

英文原文: Zestea sits overlooking the Sethys River as it flows into the Perassic Sea. The conjoining of the waters makes for rich fishing grounds, for the shad and sardines who thrive in the brackish estuary waters as well as the tuna who come to eat them.
中文译文: 泽斯特亚坐落在俯瞰塞西斯河汇入佩拉西海之处。两水交汇形成了丰富的渔场，既有在咸水河口繁衍生息的西鲱和沙丁鱼，也有前来捕食它们的金枪鱼。

英文原文: The natural fortress of Lycaron sits on a spur jutting out of the great Ornian rock. In the final years of the imperial conquest, the Palaic tribe here negotiated a peaceful federation with the Empire, only to see their home seized by the rebel leader Gethuz. The ensuing siege was only the first of many that Lycaron would undergo in the ensuing centuries, as its nearby silver mines made it a prize for every imperial pretender who ever tried to seize power in a civil war. It is said that the colony of vultures who inhabit the upper reaches of the Rock have dined on the flesh of every people of Calradia.
中文译文: 莱卡隆这座天然堡垒坐落在从巨大的奥尔尼安岩伸出的山嘴上。在帝国征服的最后几年，这里的帕莱克部落与帝国谈判达成了和平联盟，却眼睁睁看着自己的家园被叛军领袖格苏兹夺取。随后的围攻只是莱卡隆在接下来几个世纪中经历的众多围攻中的第一次，因为其附近的银矿使其成为每一位在内战中试图夺取权力的帝国僭位者的战利品。据说，栖息在岩石上层的秃鹫群曾以卡拉迪亚所有民族的血肉为食。

英文原文: Sagora sits on a low ridge overlooking the River Sethys. This district is one of the rockier and less fertile parts of the river valley, but the valley scrub still supports the grazing of sheep.
中文译文: 萨戈拉坐落在俯瞰塞西斯河的低矮山脊上。这个地区是河谷中较为多石且不那么肥沃的部分之一，但河谷灌木丛仍然可以放牧羊群。

英文原文: Popsia lies on the eastern side of Mount Tarnea. Pelaic myth says that two gigantic brothers fought here and killed each other. Their sister Tarnea buried one in the depths of the Ophthys, the other in Lake Laconis, then transformed herself into the mountain to weep over their graves. Trees grow tall here in the cool shadows of the heights, and villagers harvest pines for sale as lumber.
中文译文: 波普西亚位于塔尔尼亚山的东侧。佩莱克神话中说，两个巨人兄弟在此战斗并杀死了对方。他们的姐妹塔尔尼亚将一人埋葬在奥菲斯深渊，另一人埋葬在拉科尼斯湖，然后将自己化为山脉，在它们的坟墓上哭泣。在高处凉爽的阴影中，树木长得高大，村民们收获松树作为木材出售。

英文原文: Canterion lies by the Sethys River, between the Ornian Rock and the Thymian Hills. The rich grasses have made this land good horse country since even before the coming of the Empire, and today the village gives its name to the chargers prized by the imperial nobility.
中文译文: 坎特里昂坐落在塞西斯河畔，介于奥尔尼安岩和提米安山丘之间。即使在帝国到来之前，丰茂的草地就使这片土地成为优良的马场，如今这个村庄的名字被赋予了帝国贵族珍视的战马。

英文原文: The tale of Sarpea of Onira is one of the epic poems of the eastern frontier. Like many, it seems written to justify the Empire's conquest.
