中文译文: 要尝试登船，将视角转向靠近你船的敌舰，并按{ATTEMPT_BOARDING_KEY}。

英文原文: To give orders to your troops, select your own formation by pressing {FIRST_GROUP_HEAR_KEY} and select the order you want.{newline}{newline}Press {THIRD_ORDER_CATEGORY_KEY} to try “Charge” now!
中文译文: 要向部队下达命令，按{FIRST_GROUP_HEAR_KEY}选择你的编队，然后选择你想要的命令。{newline}{newline}按{THIRD_ORDER_CATEGORY_KEY}尝试“冲锋”！

英文原文: To give orders to your troops, open the formation selection by holding {HOLD_OPEN_ORDER_KEY} and select the order you want.{newline}{newline}Press {FIRST_ORDER_CATEGORY_KEY} and then {THIRD_ORDER_CATEGORY_KEY} to try “Charge” now!
中文译文: 要向部队下达命令，按住{HOLD_OPEN_ORDER_KEY}打开编队选择，然后选择你想要的命令。{newline}{newline}按{FIRST_ORDER_CATEGORY_KEY}再按{THIRD_ORDER_CATEGORY_KEY}尝试“冲锋”！

英文原文: When your ship is connected to other ships, you can issue a cut loose order by pressing {CUT_LOOSE_KEY} while you are in direct control.
中文译文: 当你的舰船与其他舰船连接时，你可以在直接操控下按{CUT_LOOSE_KEY}发出卡特·卢斯命令。
科曼丁·弗兰德利·希普斯

英文原文: You can command friendly ships, as long as you have the suitable authority.{newline}{newline}To command a ship to attack an enemy ship, press {SHIP_COMMANDING_TUTORIAL_GROUP_KEY} and then press {FOURTH_ORDER_CATEGORY_KEY} issue the engage order.
中文译文: 只要你有合适的权限，就可以指挥友舰。{newline}{newline}要命令一艘舰船攻击敌舰，按{SHIP_COMMANDING_TUTORIAL_GROUP_KEY}再按{FOURTH_ORDER_CATEGORY_KEY}下达接战命令。

英文原文: You can command friendly ships, as long as you have the suitable authority.{newline}{newline}To command a ship to attack an enemy ship, hold {HOLD_OPEN_ORDER_KEY}, use the {SELECT_LEFT_FORMATION_KEY}/{SELECT_RIGHT_FORMATION_KEY} and {APPLY_SELECTION_KEY} to select an allied ship. Use {FIRST_ORDER_CATEGORY_KEY} to choose movement orders and press {FOURTH_ORDER_CATEGORY_KEY} issue the engage order.
中文译文: 只要你有合适的权限，就可以指挥友舰。{newline}{newline}要命令一艘舰船攻击敌舰，按住{HOLD_OPEN_ORDER_KEY}，使用{SELECT_LEFT_FORMATION_KEY}/{SELECT_RIGHT_FORMATION_KEY}和{APPLY_SELECTION_KEY}选择一艘友舰。使用{FIRST_ORDER_CATEGORY_KEY}选择移动命令，并按{FOURTH_ORDER_CATEGORY_KEY}下达接战命令。

中文译文: 舰船可投入战斗的部队数。

中文译文: 舰船可携带的增援部队数。

英文原文: The maximum number of troops a ship can carry without travel speed penalties.
中文译文: 舰船在不影响航行速度的前提下可搭载的最大部队数。

英文原文: Inventory capacity the ship provides.{newline}{newline}Going above this limit will cause travel speed penalties.

英文原文: How heavy the ship is.{newline}{newline}Lighter ships are easier to maneuver and accelerate, but have more trouble in rough seas.{newline}{newline}Heavier ships are harder to accelerate, but handle rough seas better and deliver more devastating ramming attacks.
中文译文: 舰船的重量。{newline}{newline}轻型舰船更易操控与加速，但在恶劣海况下更易受损。{newline}{newline}重型舰船加速较慢，但能更好地应对恶劣海况，且撞击攻击更具毁灭性。

英文原文: Sail type of the ship.{newline}{newline}Square sails provide higher acceleration, with moderate top speeds and limited wind coverage.{newline}{newline}Lateen sails provide low acceleration, with higher top speeds and wider wind coverage.{newline}{newline}Ships with both sail types have good acceleration, wind coverage and top speeds.
中文译文: 舰船帆型。{newline}{newline}方阵帆提供更高加速度，中等极速，风向覆盖有限。{newline}{newline}三角帆提供较低加速度，更高极速，风向覆盖更广。{newline}{newline}同时具备两种帆型的舰船，加速度、风向覆盖与极速均表现良好。

英文原文: Draft type of the ship.{newline}{newline}Ships with shallow drafts travel faster through rivers and coastal seas, and slower through open waters.{newline}{newline}Ships with deep drafts travel slower through rivers and coastal seas, and faster through open waters.
中文译文: 舰船吃水类型。{newline}{newline}浅吃水舰船在河流与近海航行更快，在开阔水域较慢。{newline}{newline}深吃水舰船在河流与近海航行较慢，在开阔水域更快。

英文原文: Resilience of the ship against the elements.{newline}{newline}Ships with high seaworthiness take less attrition on open sea, and can even handle light storms.
中文译文: 舰船抵御风浪的韧性。{newline}{newline}高适航性的舰船在开阔海域损耗更少，甚至能应对轻度风暴。

英文原文: When a broad, low, fine-lined square-sailed vessel is spotted near a northern coastal village, an experienced mariner is usually summoned to look out from a cliff and make a critical determination: is it a snekkja or drakkar packed with Nord warriors? In that case, the people must gather their herds and valuables and rush to the nearest hiding spot in the woods. Or is it a knarr? In that case, the villagers pack up their trade goods and hurry to the nearest town, ready to bargain for walrus ivory, whale oil and other treasures of the north, hauled by this sturdy commercial vessel used by various peoples across the northern seas.
中文译文: 当一艘船体宽阔、低矮、线条流畅的方塞尔船出现在北方沿海村庄附近时，通常会召唤一位经验丰富的马里纳号从悬崖上瞭望，做出关键判断：那是一艘载满诺德战士的斯内克贾号还是龙头战船？若是如此，人们必须聚集牲畜和财物，迅速逃往林中最近的藏身处。或者，那是一艘克纳尔号？若是如此，村民们便打包贸易品，赶往最近的城镇，准备交易沃尔鲁斯号象牙、鲸油和其他北方特雷热号——这些货物由这艘坚固的商船运载，航行于北方海域的各民族之间。

英文原文: The dhow, and ships built on the same lines, have been sailing the seas of southern Calradia for over a thousand years, its distinctive silhouette appearing in carvings on Kannic tomb walls. The triangular lateen sail that is now almost universally carried by dhows is a more recent invention, allowing these ships to catch seasonal winds blowing out of the Nahhasa desert or the mountains to their north parallel to the main trade routes.
中文译文: 三角帆船及其同型船只已在卡拉迪亚南部海域航行千年之久，其独特轮廓出现在坎尼克墓壁的雕刻中。如今三角帆船几乎普遍采用的三角拉丁塞尔是较晚的发明，使这些船只能够捕捉纳哈萨沙漠吹来的季节性温德号，或与其北部山脉平行的主要贸易路线上的山风。

英文原文: The Nords claim that the god Fryr taught their people the secret of the lapstrake, a technique of shipbuilding in which planks overlap each other to create a strong, light hull. Inspection of imperial annals might site this mythic event to just sometime near the year 700, when longships full of warriors first began to terrorize the seas of the north. The drakkar or dragon-ship is the pinnacle of this design, the pride of any jarl’s fleet. Nord chiefs lavish gold on bards who sing of the sleek lines of their drakkars, in the same way an eastern lord might pay a poet to praise his horses. That said, they are smaller than the flagships of most of their adversaries, and usually mount little in the way of ballistae or other auxiliary weapons. Ships like the dromon are made by engineers, who wish to apply the Empire’s methodical battle-winning arts to combat at sea. The drakkar in contrast is a ship for heroes, warriors who want to get to their foeman’s ship, scramble aboard, and start putting axes into skulls.
中文译文: 诺德人声称，神祇弗莱尔教会了他们搭接船壳的秘密——一种船板重叠以建造坚固轻巧哈尔的造船技术。查阅帝国编年史，这一神话事件可能发生在接近700年的某个时候，当时满载战士的长船首次开始肆虐北方海域。龙头战船，或称龙船，是这种设计的巅峰之作，任何雅尔的舰队之骄傲。诺德酋长们挥金如土，赏赐吟唱其龙头战船流畅线条的诗人，正如东方雅尔可能付钱给诗人赞美其骏马。尽管如此，它们比大多数对手的旗舰要小，通常只配备少量巴利斯塔或其他辅助武器。像德罗蒙战舰这样的船只由工程师建造，他们希望将帝国系统化的制胜技艺应用于海战。而龙头战船则是为英雄和战士准备的船只——他们渴望冲上敌船，攀爬登舰，将战斧劈入颅骨。

英文原文: Several centuries ago, northern shipbuilders developed a new technique of hullbuilding in which the planks of the hull overlapped each other. They produced light, strong warships with fine lines that could be built quickly but were still seaworthy enough for the harsh northern ocean. This allowed peoples like the Nords, the Vlandians and others to range far from their homelands, trading, raiding, or fighting as mercenaries. The light longship, known to the Nords as the snekkja or snake, is the smallest of these vessels, the kind that might be outfitted by a lone band of raiders, or cover the flanks of a jarl’s fleet.
中文译文: 几个世纪前，北方造船匠发展出一种新的哈尔建造技术，哈尔板相互重叠。他们制造出莱特、斯特朗、线条流畅的战舰，建造迅速，且足以抵御严酷的北方海洋。这使得诺德人、瓦兰迪亚人及其他民族能够远离故土，进行贸易、劫掠或作为雇佣兵作战。莱特长船，诺德人称之为斯内克贾号或蛇船，是其中最小的船只，可能由一队孤立的掠夺者装备，或掩护雅尔舰队的侧翼。

英文原文: The qalguk is a product of a very different shipbuilding tradition than other heavy warships, that of the cities on the eastern edge of Lake Tanaesis. Until recently, both sides of the lake were part of the Empire, and there was little need for ships that could stand in a battle-line. The coming of the Khuzaits from the eastern steppe however brought war to Tanaesis, and the khans demanded fleets that were the equal of any other realm from his new subjects. What they received was the qalguk, a warship made by shipwrights who learned their trade building merchantmen and pleasure-craft for the great lake. Compared to other heavies it is delicate, and finicky, but it can outrun virtually any other vessel on the water. Despite the limitations, the khans appreciated a ship that gave them the same advantage at sea as their horses did on land: the choice to avoid hand-to-handing fighting and pepper the enemy with arrows, or, if they thought they had an advantage, to close quickly and strike a devastating blow.
中文译文: 卡尔古克是源自塔纳埃西斯湖东岸城市的一种截然不同的造船传统的产物。直到最近，湖的两岸都是帝国的一部分，几乎没有需要能列阵作战的船只。然而，库赛特人从东方草原的到来给塔纳埃西斯带来了战争，可汗要求其新臣民提供与其他王国匹敌的舰队。他们得到的便是卡尔古克——一种由学习建造商船和游船为生的造船匠建造的战舰。与其他赫维号相比，它精致而挑剔，但在水面上几乎能超越任何其他船只。尽管有局限，可汗们欣赏这种船只在海上给予他们与陆上马匹相同的优势：选择避免肉搏，用箭雨骚扰敌人，或者，若认为有优势，则迅速接近并施以毁灭性打击。

英文原文: The dromakion is a somewhat smaller version of the dromon. The keel and frame do not bear anywhere near as much weight as the dromon, and do not require the building techniques that are jealously guarded by imperial shipwrights. Consequently, it is in wide use across south and east Calradia, by Aserai, Khuzaits and the more cost-conscious imperial archons.
中文译文: 德罗马基翁是德罗蒙战舰的较小版本。其龙骨和框架远不及德罗蒙战舰沉重，也不需要帝国造船匠严密守护的建造技术。因此，它在卡拉迪亚南部和东部广泛使用，阿塞莱人、库赛特人以及更注重成本的帝国执政官均使用此船。

英文原文: Corbitae have been hauling the cargo of the Calradian Empire for over a millennium. Their holds, more capacious than those of any other ship on the sea, were designed to ferry vast quantities of grain from the warm lands around the Perassic Sea to the sprawling imperial capital at Paravenos. The empire is no longer as centralized as it once was, and none of its cities now require the bounty of a continent to feed them, but corbitae are still the engine of commerce across the imperial-controlled northern shores of the Perassic.
中文译文: 柯比塔船已为卡拉迪亚帝国运输货物超过千年。其货舱比海上任何其他船只都更宽敞，专为将大量谷物从佩拉西克海周围的温暖地区运往庞大的帝国首都帕拉维诺斯而设计。帝国已不再像过去那样集权，其城市也不再需要整个大陆的物产来供养，但柯比塔船仍是帝国控制的佩拉西克北部海岸贸易的引擎。

英文原文: The dromon is the work of imperial shipwrights who have taken the heavy galleys of the early empire and updated them with the technologies of the seafaring peoples of the south and east. Their unusual sail plan, a lateen in front and a squaresail behind, reflects this hybrid tradition. The name means “runner”, but nautical design and terminology evolve differently, and the dromon is distinguished not by its speed but by being the heaviest and sturdiest ship on the five seas. Like all heavy warships they turn and accelerate slowly, and thus must rely on smaller craft like the liburna to chase nimble pirates and smugglers. But in battle a line of dromons, their rams cutting the waters ahead of them, epitomizes the irresistible might of the Empire.
中文译文: 德罗蒙战舰是帝国造船匠的杰作，他们以早期帝国的重型桨帆船为基础，融合南方与东方航海民族的技术加以革新。其独特的帆装——前挂三角帆、后悬方帆——正体现了这种混血传统。其名意为“奔跑者”，但船舶设计与术语的演变各有路径，德罗蒙并非以速度著称，而是以五海之上最厚重坚固的船体闻名。如同所有重型战舰，它转向与加速迟缓，因此需依赖利布尔纳之类的小型舰船追捕敏捷的海盗与走私者。然而在战场上，一列德罗蒙战舰排开，撞角劈波斩浪，正是帝国不可抗拒之力的化身。

英文原文: The small fishing boats used in coastal villages throughout Calradia are generally quite similar to each other. Most are designed simply to sail to the nearest fishing grounds, lay out nets, and sail back with as big catch as possible, though some can be used to chase and harpoon smaller whales.
中文译文: 卡拉迪亚沿海村庄使用的小型渔船彼此大同小异。多数船只的设计仅是为了驶往最近渔场、撒网、然后满载而归，不过有些也可用于追逐并叉捕小型鲸鱼。

英文原文: This galley is used throughout the western coasts of Calradia for both commerce and warfare. It is an evolution of the vessels used by mariners from the Biscan, Byalic and Nordvyg coasts to fish, whale and trade. This galley is flexible and relatively cheap to build, making it the workhorse of Battanian, Vlandian and imperial seafarers. It mounts the square sail that is generally preferred by seafarers in northern waters.
中文译文: 这种桨帆船在卡拉迪亚西海岸广泛用于商贸与作战。它由比斯坎、比亚利克和诺德维格海岸的水手们捕鱼、捕鲸和贸易所用的船只演变而来。这种船灵活且造价相对低廉，成为巴旦尼亚、瓦兰迪亚和帝国航海者的主力船型。它悬挂方帆，这是北方水域水手普遍偏好的帆型。

英文原文: The lodya draws two shipbuilding traditions: that of the Nords who sailed their longships to trade and raid up Sturgian rivers, and that of the great galleys that sailed from imperial ports on Lake Laconis, whose glory the Sturgian princes wished to surpass. In its basic lines it resembles a sleek shallow-drafted river-craft, with ample oar-power to row against the wind or current, but is much larger. Sturgians however often arm it for battle like a dromon, packing its well-protected decks with archers and sometimes even mounting a ram.
中文译文: 洛迪亚船融合了两大造船传统：一是诺德人驾驶长船沿斯特吉亚河流贸易与劫掠的传统，二是从帝国拉科尼斯湖港口驶出的巨型桨帆船的传统——斯特吉亚王子们渴望超越后者的荣光。其基本线型类似一艘线条流畅、吃水浅的河船，配备充足的桨力以逆风或逆流航行，但船体远为庞大。然而斯特吉亚人常将其武装如德罗蒙战舰，在防护良好的甲板上满载弓箭手，有时甚至加装撞角。

英文原文: The Battanians are less known for their maritime traditions than many other peoples of the north, but some tribes have long plied the rivers and lakes of their forest lands or traded along the coast. The birlinn, or “catship”, evolved out of the smaller flat-bottomed boats used on inland waters. Its low freeboard means that it struggles in rough seas, but in a calm or a swell its stubby hull is perhaps the most maneuverable of any Calradian vessel, adept for darting around rocks, whirlpools or shoals.
中文译文: 巴旦尼亚人的航海传统虽不及北方其他民族闻名，但有些部落长久以来在其森林地带的河流湖泊上行船，或沿近海贸易。比林船，或称“猫船”，由内陆水域使用的小型平底船演变而来。其干舷低矮，在汹涌海况中挣扎，但在风平浪静或涌浪中，其短粗船体或许是所有卡拉迪亚船只中最灵活的，善于在礁石、漩涡或浅滩间穿梭。

英文原文: The ghurab, or “crow”, is the Aserai sultanate’s answer to the dromon. The ports on the south coast of the Perassic Sea, once home to the Kannic states, have a long tradition of shipbuilding. Most of their craft, however, were light and swift. In centuries passed, when ramming was decisive, they served the Kannic navies well. Today however missile fire and boarding decides most battles, and the emirs who now rule this coast felt a need for bigger and stronger vessels.
中文译文: 古拉布船，或称“鸦船”，是阿塞莱苏丹国对德罗蒙战舰的回应。佩拉斯海（注：原文Perassic Sea，根据上下文音译）南岸的港口曾是卡尼克诸国的家园，拥有悠久的造船传统。然而，他们的大多数船只轻快迅捷。在过去的几个世纪里，当撞击战术决定胜负时，它们为卡尼克海军立下汗马功劳。但如今，远程射击与接舷战主导了大多数海战，统治这片海岸的埃米尔们感到需要更大更强的战舰。

英文原文: The liburna is a centuries-old design, but it is still effective. Like many traditional imperial warships, it carries a ram. These are less decisive than they were centuries ago, when hulls were more brittle, but still can cripple or sink an opponent with several blows. They serve as the workhorses of the imperial navy, able to serve as scout and pursuit ships and as auxiliaries to the battle-line of dromons.
中文译文: 利布尔纳战船的设计已有数百年历史，但至今依然有效。如同许多传统帝国战舰，它装有撞角。这些撞角虽不如几个世纪前船体更脆弱时那般具有决定性，但仍可通过数次撞击使对手瘫痪或沉没。它们作为帝国海军的主力舰船，既能充当侦察与追击舰，也能作为德罗蒙战列线的辅助舰只。

英文原文: Made and used in abundance in northern seas, longships are seaworthy, light in weight, easy to sail, quick to gain speed and turn. Sailing low on water, and with its shallow draft, longships can navigate rivers and shore with ease, but have trouble in open seas. In battle, Longships try to bring their large crew into boarding action as soon as possible, which is not a hard task due its many oars.
中文译文: 长船在北方海域大量制造和使用，它们适航性强、重量轻、易于航行、加速快且转向灵活。吃水浅、干舷低，长船能轻松穿梭于河流与近岸，但在公海则力有不逮。战斗中，长船力求尽快将庞大的船员投入博丁，因其桨数众多，这并非难事。

英文原文: Light Longships, fitting as many sailers and oars into its small hull as possible, are dangerous both to enemies and it’s own crew. It is agile enough to avoid ramming, or ballista shots but it can not take more than a few hits.
中文译文: 莱特长船在狭小的哈尔内尽可能容纳更多水手与桨，对敌我双方都构成危险。它足够灵活，能避开撞角或巴利斯塔射击，但承受不了几次打击。

英文原文: The cog was developed by shipwrights on the western coasts of Calradia to travel long distances and weather the fierce storms of the ocean. Its ancestors were similar to the Nordic knarr or the Battanian birlinn, but the cog evolved to become much taller, broader and heavier. It serves both as a trading vessel and as a warship in the navies of the Vlandian and Battanian lords who now rule these coasts, the latter usually mounting a stern castle.
中文译文: 柯克号由卡拉迪亚西海岸的造船匠开发，用于远航并抵御海洋的狂暴风暴。其祖先类似诺德人的克纳尔号或巴旦尼亚人的伯林，但柯克号演变得更高大、更宽厚、更沉重。它既作为商船，也作为战舰服役于如今统治这些海岸的瓦兰迪亚和巴旦尼亚领主的舰队中，后者通常加装艉楼。

英文原文: This galley is common in southern and eastern Calradia, in the more restricted waters of the Gulf of Charas, the Perassic Sea and Lake Tanaesis. It evolved from the swift galleys that the Kannic states used to contest the seas with the Calradian Empire in its infancy. Today, Aserai, Khuzaits and imperial seafarers use it for both trade and warfare. It mounts the lateen sail, which is preferred by sailors from these lands.
中文译文: 这种桨帆船常见于卡拉迪亚南部和东部，即查拉斯湾、佩拉斯海与塔纳埃西斯湖等较为受限的水域。它由卡尼克诸国在卡拉迪亚帝国初创时期用于争夺海权的快速桨帆船演变而来。如今，阿塞莱人、库赛特人和帝国航海者将其用于贸易与作战。它悬挂塞尔，这是这些地区水手偏爱的帆型。

英文原文: Despite being a clinker ship, Cogs are still taller and sturdier from northern ships. It can carry a decent crew and reinforcements, and sail at a decent pace. Cogs can hold the line against many other ships, and used in western navies for that purpose.
中文译文: 尽管是叠接船，柯克号仍比北方舰船更高大坚固。它能搭载可观的水手与援军，并以不错的速度航行。柯克号能抵御众多敌船，西方海军常以此为目的使用之。

英文原文: When Vlandians arrived on the shores of Calradia they sailed in simple craft similar to Nord longships. But, just as the first generations of Vlandian settlers learned to build fortifications to protect their new lands, so they also learned to build citadels upon the waves, the great roundships, to protect the commerce that enriched them. Their basic lines are similar to those of cogs common in Vlandian ports but the roundship is nearly twice as large, mounting two large square sails. They are designed to make full use of the Vlandian tradition of crossbowmanship, with castles at both bow and stern providing the marines a lofty point from which to shoot as well as cover to reload.
中文译文: 当弗兰迪亚人抵达卡拉迪亚海岸时，他们驾驶着类似诺德长船的简陋船只。但正如第一代弗兰迪亚定居者学会修建堡垒以保护新土地，他们也学会了在波涛上建造堡垒——大型圆船——以保护滋养他们的贸易。其基本线型与弗兰迪亚港口常见的柯克号相似，但圆船几乎大两倍，装有两面大方塞尔。它们旨在充分利用弗兰迪亚人的弩术传统，船首与船尾的船楼为海军陆战队员提供高耸的射击点与装填掩护。

英文原文: Throws a wooden bolt, effective against crews and deals light damage to ships.
中文译文: 发射木制弩箭，对船员有效，对舰船造成轻微伤害。

英文原文: Throws a burning wooden bolt, effective against crews. Fire damage can burn sails and light ships.
中文译文: 发射燃烧的木制弩箭，对船员有效。火焰伤害可烧毁塞尔并点燃舰船。

英文原文: Throws a heavy stone shot, very good at dealing damage to ships.
中文译文: 发射重型石弹，对舰船造成极高伤害。

英文原文: Throws a large fire pot, dealing large amount fire damage burning ships and sails. Very effective against crews.
中文译文: 投掷大型火罐，造成大量火焰伤害，烧毁舰船与塞尔。对船员极为有效。

英文原文: Throws a heavy iron shot, excellent at dealing damage to ships.
中文译文: 发射赫维号铁弹，对舰船造成卓越伤害。
格雷普肖特巴利斯塔号

英文原文: Throws multiple small stones, very effective againts crews.
中文译文: 散射多枚小石子，对船员极为有效。

英文原文: Throws multiple small burning stones, very effective againts crews. Fire damage can burn sails and light ships.
中文译文: 散射多枚燃烧的小石子，对船员极为有效。火焰伤害可烧毁塞尔并点燃舰船。

英文原文: Forecastle protects troops and strengthens the hull, at a cost of additional weight.
中文译文: 船首楼保护部队并加固哈尔，但增加额外重量。

英文原文: Forecastle protects troops and strengthens the hull, at a cost of additional weight. Barracks allows more crew on board.
中文译文: 船首楼保护部队并加固哈尔，但增加额外重量。兵营允许搭载更多船员。

英文原文: Reinforced forecastle protects troops and strengthens the hull, at a cost of additional weight. Barracks allows more crew on board.
中文译文: 强化船首楼保护部队并加固哈尔，但增加额外重量。兵营允许搭载更多船员。

英文原文: Aftcastle protects troops and strengthens the hull, at a cost of additional weight.
中文译文: 阿夫特楼保护部队并加固哈尔，但增加额外重量。
巴特尔门茨·威斯·巴拉克

英文原文: Aftcastle protects troops and strengthens the hull, at a cost of additional weight. Barracks allows more troops on board.
中文译文: 阿夫特楼保护部队并加固哈尔，但增加额外重量。兵营允许搭载更多部队。

英文原文: Reinforced aftcastle protects troops and strengthens the hull, at a cost of additional weight. Barracks allows more troops on board.
中文译文: 强化阿夫特楼保护部队并加固哈尔，但增加额外重量。兵营允许搭载更多部队。
巴特尔门茨·威斯·因普鲁夫德·拉德

英文原文: Aftcastle protects troops and strengthens the hull, at a cost of additional weight. Improved rudder increases maneuverability.
中文译文: 阿夫特楼保护部队并加固哈尔，但增加额外重量。改进舵提升机动性。

英文原文: Reinforced aftcastle protects troops and strengthens the hull, at a cost of additional weight. Improved rudder increases maneuverability.
中文译文: 强化阿夫特楼保护部队并加固哈尔，但增加额外重量。改进舵提升机动性。

英文原文: Wavebreaker ram can deal heavy blows to enemy ships, demoralize their crews while improving speed, at a cost of additional weight.
中文译文: 韦夫布雷克罗姆号可重创敌舰船、打击敌人士气，同时提升速度，但增加额外重量。

英文原文: Reinforced wavebreaker ram strengthens the hull and can deal heavy blows to enemy ships, demoralize their crews while improving speed, at a cost of aditional weight.
中文译文: 强化韦夫布雷克罗姆号加固哈尔，可重创敌舰船、打击其船员士气，同时提升速度，但增加额外重量。

英文原文: Ram can deal damaging blows enemy ships and demoralize their crews, at a cost of additional weight.
中文译文: 撞角可重创敌舰船并打击其船员士气，但增加额外重量。

英文原文: Heavy ram can deal devastating blows enemy ships and demoralize their crews, at a cost of additional weight.
中文译文: 赫维号撞角可毁灭性重创敌舰船并打击其船员士气，但增加额外重量。
赫维雷因福斯特拉姆号

英文原文: Reinforced heavy ram strengthens the hull and can deal devastating blows enemy ships and demoralize their crews, at a cost of additional weight.
中文译文: 强化赫维号撞角加固哈尔，可毁灭性重创敌舰船并打击其船员士气，但增加额外重量。

英文原文: Protects the hull from elements, at a cost of additional weight.
中文译文: 保护哈尔免受风雨侵蚀，但增加额外重量。

英文原文: Strengthens the hull and protects it from elements, at a cost of additional weight.
中文译文: 加固哈尔并保护其免受风雨侵蚀，但增加额外重量。

英文原文: Strengthens the hull and provide excellent protection against elements, at a cost of additional weight.
中文译文: 加固哈尔，提供极佳防护，但增加额外重量。

英文原文: Provides excellent protection against elements, at a cost of a small additonal weight.
中文译文: 提供极佳防护，但增加少量额外重量。

英文原文: Provides excellent protection against elements and improves ship speed, at cost of a small additonal weight.
中文译文: 提供极佳防护并提升舰船速度，但增加少量额外重量。

英文原文: Placed at sides of the ship, provides cover and some protection from arrows
中文译文: 置于舰船侧舷两侧，提供掩体与少量箭矢防护。

英文原文: Placed at sides of the ship, provides cover and moderate protection from arrows
中文译文: 置于舰船侧舷两侧，提供掩体与中等箭矢防护。

英文原文: Placed at sides of the ship, provides cover and excellent protection from arrows
中文译文: 置于舰船侧舷两侧，提供掩体与极佳箭矢防护。

英文原文: Supplies crew with fire arrows, dealing fire damage to enemy ships and sails.
中文译文: 为船员提供火箭，对敌舰船与塞尔造成火焰伤害。

英文原文: Stores small amount of additional cargo on deck.
中文译文: 在德克上存放少量额外货物。
在德克上存放额外货物。

英文原文: Stores large amount of additional cargo on deck.
中文译文: 在德克上存放大量额外货物。

英文原文: Protects the ship against elements, and stores small mount of additional cargo.
中文译文: 保护舰船免受风雨侵蚀，并存放少量额外货物。

英文原文: Excellent at protecting the ship against elements, and stores small mount of additional cargo.
中文译文: 极佳地保护舰船免受风雨侵蚀，并存放少量额外货物。

英文原文: Weapons and tools to improve crew in melee combat.
中文译文: 武器与工具，提升船员近战能力。

英文原文: Sharpened weapons and tools to improve crew in melee combat.
中文译文: 锋利的武器与工具，提升船员近战能力。

英文原文: Fine honed weapons and tools to improve crew in melee combat.
中文译文: 精磨的武器与工具，提升船员近战能力。

英文原文: Small barrels filled with arrows and bolts to restock archers during battles.
中文译文: 装满箭矢的小桶，用于战斗中补充弓箭手。

英文原文: Crates filled with arrows and bolts to restock archers during battles.
中文译文: 装满箭矢的板条箱，用于战斗中补充弓箭手。

英文原文: Large crates filled with arrows and bolts to restock archers during battles.
中文译文: 装满箭矢的大板条箱，用于战斗中补充弓箭手。

英文原文: Small barrels filled with quivers and throwing weapons to restock crew during battles.
中文译文: 装满箭袋与投掷武器的小桶，用于战斗中补充船员。
拉奇阿罗安德贾夫林克雷茨号

英文原文: Large crates filled with quivers and throwing weapons to restock crew during battles.
中文译文: 装满箭袋与投掷武器的大板条箱，用于战斗中补充船员。

英文原文: Light oars can be rowed faster, increasing top oar speed.
中文译文: 莱特桨可以划得更快，提升最高桨速。

英文原文: Aspen light oars can be rowed faster, increasing top oar speed.
中文译文: 阿斯彭·莱特·奥尔斯可以划得更快，提升最高桨速。

英文原文: Ash light oars can be rowed faster, increasing top oar speed.
中文译文: 阿什·莱特·奥尔斯可以划得更快，提升最高桨速。

英文原文: Wide oars provide more force with each pull, improving maneuverability.
中文译文: 怀德奥尔斯号每次划动提供更大力量，提升机动性。

英文原文: Birch wide oars provide more force with each pull, improving maneuverability.
中文译文: 伯奇·怀德·奥尔斯每次划动提供更大力量，提升机动性。

英文原文: Oaken wide oars provide more force with each pull, improving maneuverability.
中文译文: 橡木宽桨每划提供更强动力，提升舰船机动性。

英文原文: Linen sails provide higher sail power, they are also lighter and easier to work.
中文译文: 莱嫩帆提供更高帆力，且更莱特易操作。

英文原文: Cotton sails have greater sail power, they are also lighter and easier to work.
中文译文: 科顿·塞尔斯提供更高帆力，且更莱特易操作。
